   let metric_families = self.registry.gather();\n722\t        let mut buffer = Vec::new();\n723\t        encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer)?;\n724\t        Ok(String::from_utf8(buffer).map_err(|e| {\n725\t            prometheus::Error::Msg(format!(\"failed to convert metrics to UTF-8: {}\", e))\n726\t        })?)\n727\t    }\n728\t\n729\t    pub fn admin_session_key_generated(&self) -> Gauge {\n730\t        self.admin_session_key_generated.clone()\n731\t    }\n732\t\n733\t    pub fn admin_session_revoked_total(&self) -> Counter {\n734\t        self.admin_session_revoked_total.clone()\n735\t    }\n736\t}\n737\t\n738\t/// Generate a short request ID from UUIDv7.\n739\t///\n740\t/// UUIDv7 provides time-ordered unique IDs. We take the first 8 hex characters,\n741\t/// hash them, and encode as hex for a short, URL-safe identifier.\n742\tpub fn generate_request_id() -> String {\n743\t    let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n744\t    let uuid_str = uuid.simple().to_string();\n745\t    // Take first 8 chars (enough entropy for uniqueness)\n746\t    let prefix = &uuid_str[..8];\n747\t\n748\t    // Hash to get a consistent short representation\n749\t    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n750\t    prefix.hash(&mut hasher);\n751\t    let hash = hasher.finish();\n752\t\n753\t    // Encode as hex (16 chars = 64 bits)\n754\t    format!(\"{:016x}\", hash)\n755\t}\n756\t\n757\t/// Extension trait to add request ID extraction utilities.\n758\tpub trait RequestIdExt {\n759\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String>;\n760\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str);\n761\t}\n762\t\n763\timpl RequestIdExt for HeaderMap {\n764\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String> {\n765\t        self.get(\"x-request-id\")\n766\t            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n767\t            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n768\t    }\n769\t\n770\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str) {\n771\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(id) {\n772\t            self.insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n773\t        }\n774\t    }\n775\t}\n776\t\n777\t/// Guard that decrements the in-flight gauge when dropped.\n778\t///\n779\t/// This ensures that even if the handler panics, the in-flight count\n780\t/// is accurately decremented.\n781\tstruct InFlightGuard {\n782\t    metrics: Metrics,\n783\t}\n784\t\n785\timpl InFlightGuard {\n786\t    fn new(metrics: Metrics) -> Self {\n787\t        metrics.requests_in_flight.inc();\n788\t        tracing::trace!(requests_in_flight = metrics.requests_in_flight.get(), \"request started\");\n789\t        Self { metrics }\n790\t    }\n791\t}\n792\t\n793\timpl Drop for InFlightGuard {\n794\t    fn drop(&mut self) {\n795\t        self.metrics.requests_in_flight.dec();\n796\t        tracing::trace!(requests_in_flight = self.metrics.requests_in_flight.get(), \"request completed\");\n797\t    }\n798\t}\n799\t\n800\t/// Extract the path template from the matched route.\n801\t///\n802\t/// Axum's MatchedPath extractor provides the route template (e.g., \"/indexes/{uid}/search\")\n803\t/// instead of the actual path (e.g., \"/indexes/products/search\"), avoiding high-cardinality labels.\n804\tfn extract_path_template(request: &Request) -> String {\n805\t    request\n806\t        .extensions()\n807\t        .get::<axum::extract::MatchedPath>()\n808\t        .map(|mp| mp.as_str())\n809\t        .unwrap_or_else(|| request.uri().path())\n810\t        .to_string()\n811\t}\n812\t\n813\t/// Main middleware that combines request ID injection, structured logging, and Prometheus metrics.\n814\tpub async fn telemetry_middleware(\n815\t    State(telemetry): State<TelemetryState>,\n816\t    mut req: Request,\n817\t    next: Next,\n818\t) -> Response {\n819\t    let start = Instant::now();\n820\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n821\t    let path_template = extract_path_template(&req);\n822\t    let metrics = telemetry.metrics.clone();\n823\t    let pod_id = telemetry.pod_id.clone();\n824\t\n825\t    // Generate or extract request ID\n826\t    let request_id = req\n827\t        .headers()\n828\t        .get_request_id()\n829\t        .unwrap_or_else(generate_request_id);\n830\t    req.headers_mut().set_request_id(&request_id);\n831\t\n832\t    // Create span for structured logging with pod_id included\n833\t    let span = info_span!(\n834\t        \"request\",\n835\t        request_id = %request_id,\n836\t        pod_id = %pod_id,\n837\t        method = %method,\n838\t        path_template = %path_template,\n839\t        path = %req.uri().path(),\n840\t    );\n841\t\n842\t    let _guard = span.enter();\n843\t\n844\t    // Track in-flight requests\n845\t    let in_flight = InFlightGuard::new(metrics.clone());\n846\t\n847\t    let response = next.run(req).await;\n848\t\n849\t    drop(in_flight);\n850\t\n851\t    let status = response.status();\n852\t    let status_u16 = status.as_u16();\n853\t    let duration = start.elapsed();\n854\t\n855\t    // Record Prometheus metrics\n856\t    metrics\n857\t        .request_duration\n858\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n859\t        .observe(duration.as_secs_f64());\n860\t    metrics\n861\t        .requests_total\n862\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n863\t        .inc();\n864\t\n865\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n866\t    // Base fields: timestamp (from tracing-subscriber), level, message, duration_ms\n867\t    // Additional fields (index, node_count, estimated_hits, degraded)\n868\t    // are added by request handlers via the tracing span.\n869\t    let message = format!(\"{} {}\", method, status);\n870\t    if status.is_server_error() {\n871\t        tracing::error!(\n872\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n873\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n874\t            request_id = %request_id,\n875\t            message = %message,\n876\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n877\t            status = status_u16,\n878\t            method = %method,\n879\t            path_template = %path_template,\n880\t        );\n881\t    } else if status.is_client_error() {\n882\t        tracing::warn!(\n883\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n884\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n885\t            request_id = %request_id,\n886\t            message = %message,\n887\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n888\t            status = status_u16,\n889\t            method = %method,\n890\t            path_template = %path_template,\n891\t        );\n892\t    } else {\n893\t        tracing::info!(\n894\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n895\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n896\t            request_id = %request_id,\n897\t            message = %message,\n898\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n899\t            status = status_u16,\n900\t            method = %method,\n901\t            path_template = %path_template,\n902\t        );\n903\t    }\n904\t\n905\t    // Ensure request ID is in response headers\n906\t    let mut response = response;\n907\t    if !response.headers().contains_key(\"x-request-id\") {\n908\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(&request_id) {\n909\t            response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n910\t        }\n911\t    }\n912\t\n913\t    response\n914\t}\n915\t\n916\t/// Create the metrics router for the :9090 server.\n917\tpub fn metrics_router() -> Router<Metrics> {\n918\t    Router::new().route(\"/metrics\", get(metrics_handler))\n919\t}\n920\t\n921\t/// Handler that returns Prometheus metrics in text exposition format.\n922\tasync fn metrics_handler(State(metrics): State<Metrics>) -> String {\n923\t    match metrics.encode_metrics() {\n924\t        Ok(metrics) => metrics,\n925\t        Err(e) => {\n926\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n927\t            format!(\"# ERROR: failed to encode metrics: {}\\n\", e)\n928\t        }\n929\t    }\n930\t}\n931\t\n932\t/// Accessor methods for metrics that can be used by other parts of the application.\n933\timpl Metrics {\n934\t    // ── Scatter-gather ──\n935\t\n936\t    pub fn record_scatter_fan_out(&self, size: u64) {\n937\t        self.scatter_fan_out_size.observe(size as }\n1269\t}\n1270\t\n1271\t#[cfg(test)]\n1272\tmod tests {\n1273\t    use super::*;\n1274\t\n1275\t    #[test]\n1276\t    fn test_request_id_generation() {\n1277\t        // Generate multiple IDs to verify format\n1278\t        for _ in 0..10 {\n1279\t            let id = generate_request_id();\n1280\t\n1281\t            // IDs should be 16 hex chars (64-bit hash)\n1282\t            assert_eq!(id.len(), 16);\n1283\t\n1284\t            // IDs should be hexadecimal\n1285\t            assert!(id.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()));\n1286\t        }\n1287\t\n1288\t        // Test that different UUID prefixes produce different IDs\n1289\t        let id1 = generate_request_id();\n1290\t        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(5));\n1291\t        let id2 = generate_request_id();\n1292\t        // In production, time ensures uniqueness; test just verifies format above\n1293\t        assert_eq!(id1.len(), 16);\n1294\t        assert_eq!(id2.len(), self.rollover_documents_expired_total.clone(),\n            rollover_last_action_seconds: self.rollover_last_action_seconds.clone(),\n            canary_runs_total: self.canary_runs_total.clone(),\n            canary_latency_ms: self.canary_latency_ms.clone(),\n            canary_assertion_failures_total: self.canary_assertion_failures_total.clone(),\n            admin_ui_sessions_total: self.admin_ui_sessions_total.clone(),\n            admin_ui_action_total: self.admin_ui_action_total.clone(),\n            admin_ui_destructive_action_total: self.admin_ui_destructive_action_total.clone(),\n            explain_requests_total: self.explain_requests_total.clone(),\n            explain_warnings_total: self.explain_warnings_total.clone(),\n            explain_execute_total: self.explain_execute_total.clone(),\n            search_ui_sessions_total: self.search_ui_sessions_total.clone(),\n            search_ui_queries_total: self.search_ui_queries_total.clone(),\n            search_ui_zero_hits_total: self.search_ui_zero_hits_total.clone(),\n            search_ui_click_through_total: self.search_ui_click_through_total.clone(),\n            search_ui_p95_ms: self.search_ui_p95_ms.clone(),\n            admin_session_key_generated: self.admin_session_key_generated.clone(),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: self.admin_session_revoked_total.clone(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for Metrics {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(&MiroirConfig::default())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Metrics {\n    pub fn new(config: &MiroirConfig) -> Self {\n        let registry = Registry::new();\n\n        // ── Request metrics ──\n        let request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_request_duration_seconds\", \"Request latency in seconds\")\n                .buckets(vec![0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0]),\n            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create request_duration histogram\");\n\n        let requests_total = CounterVec::new(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_total\", \"Total number of requests\"),\n            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create requests_total counter\");\n\n        let requests_in_flight = Gauge::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_in_flight\", \"Number of requests currently being processed\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create requests_in_flight gauge\");\n\n        // ── Node health metrics ──\n        let node_healthy = GaugeVec::new(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_healthy\", \"Health status of backend nodes (1=healthy, 0=unhealthy)\"),\n            &[\"node_id\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create node_healthy gauge\");\n\n        let node_request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_node_request_duration_seconds\", \"Latency of individual node requests\")\n                .buckets(vec![0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0]),\n            &[\"node_id\", \"operation\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create node_request_duration histogram\");\n\n        let node_errors = CounterVec::new(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_errors_total\", \"Number of errors from backend nodes\"),\n            &[\"node_id\", \"error_type\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create node_errors counter\");\n\n        // ── Shard metrics ──\n        let shard_coverage = Gauge::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_coverage\", \"Fraction of shards with at least one healthy replica\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create shard_coverage gauge\");\n\n        let degraded_shards = Gauge::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_degraded_shards_total\", \"Number of shards with reduced replica availability\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create degraded_shards gauge\");\n\n        let shard_distribution = GaugeVec::new(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_distribution\", \"Number of shards assigned to each node\"),\n            &[\"node_id\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create shard_distribution gauge\");\n\n        // ── Task metrics ──\n        let task_processing_age = Histogram::with_opts(\n            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_task_processing_age_seconds\", \"Time between task creation and processing start\")\n                .buckets(vec![0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0]),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create task_processing_age histogram\");\n\n        let tasks_total = CounterVec::new(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_tasks_total\", \"Total number of tasks by status\"),\n            &[\"status\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create tasks_total counter\");\n\n        let task_registry_size = Gauge::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_task_registry_size\", \"Current number of tasks in the registry\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create task_registry_size gauge\");\n\n        // ── Scatter-gather metrics ──\n        let scatter_fan_out_size = Histogram::with_opts(\n            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_scatter_fan_out_size\", \"Number of nodes in scatter operations\")\n                .buckets(vec![1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 5.0, 10.0, 20.0, 50.0]),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_fan_out_size histogram\");\n\n        let scatter_partial_responses = Counter::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_partial_responses_total\", \"Number of scatter responses that were partial (some nodes failed)\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_partial_responses counter\");\n\n        let scatter_retries = Counter::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_retries_total\", \"Number of scatter retry attempts due to node failures\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_retries counter\");\n\n        // ── Rebalancer metrics ──\n        let rebalance_in_progress = Gauge::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_in_progress\", \"Whether a rebalance is currently running (1=yes, 0=no)\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_in_progress gauge\");\n\n        let rebalance_documents_migrated = Counter::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_documents_migrated_total\", \"Total number of documents migrated during rebalance\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_documents_migrated .buckets(vec![1.0, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0, 300.0, 600.0, 1800.0, 3600.0]),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_duration histogram\");\n\n        // Register all metrics\n        macro_rules! reg {\n            ($m:expr) => {\n                registry.register(Box::new($m.clone())).expect(concat!(\"failed to register \", stringify!($m)));\n            };\n        }\n\n        reg!(request_duration);\n        reg!(requests_total);\n        reg!(requests_in_flight);\n        reg!(node_healthy);\n        reg!(node_request_duration);\n        reg!(node_errors);\n        reg!(shard_coverage);\n        reg!(degraded_shards);\n        reg!(shard_distribution);\n        reg!(task_processing_age);\n        reg!(tasks_total);\n        reg!(task_registry_size);\n reg!(rebalance_duration);\n\n        // ── §13.11 Multi-search metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 tenants, rest bucketed) ──\n        let (\n            multisearch_queries_per_batch,\n     (None, None, None)\n        };\n\n        // ── §13.13 CDC metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 sinks, rest bucketed) ──\n        let (\n            cdc_events_published_total,\n   ).expect(\"create admin_ui_action_total\");\n            let d = CounterVec::new(\n                Opts::new(\"miroir_admin_ui_destructive_action_total\", \"Admin UI destructive actions (delete,       } else {\n            (None, None, None)\n        };\n\n        // ── §13.20 Explain metrics ──\n        let (\n            explain_requests_total,\n            explain_warnings_total,\n          ).expect(\"create explain_requests_total\");\n            let w = CounterVec::new(\n                Opts::new(\"miroir_explain_warnings_total\", \"Explain warnings by type\"),\n None, None)\n        };\n\n        // ── §13.21 Search UI metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 indexes, rest bucketed) ──\n        let (\n            search_ui_sessions_total,\n &[\"index\"],\n            ).expect(\"create search_ui_zero_hits_total\");\n            let c = CounterVec::new(\n                Opts::new(\"miroir_search_ui_click_through_total\", \"Search UI reg!(z); reg!(c); reg!(p);\n            (Some(s), Some(q), Some(z), Some(c), Some(p))\n        } else {\n            (None, None, None, None, None)\n        };\n\n        // ── Admin session sealing metrics (always present) ──\n        let admin_session_key_generated = Gauge::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_admin_session_key_generated\",\n                \"Whether ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY was generated at startup (1=yes, 0=set via env)\")\n        ).expect(\"create admin_session_key_generated\");\n        let admin_session_revoked_total = Counter::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_admin_session_revoked_total\",\n                \"Admin sessions revoked via logout\")\n        ).expect(\"create admin_session_revoked_total\");\n        reg!(admin_session_key_generated);\n        reg!(admin_session_revoked_total);\n\n        Self {\n            registry,\n            request_duration,\n            requests_total,\n            requests_in_flight,\n            node_healthy,\n            node_request_duration,\n            node_errors,\n            shard_coverage,\n            degraded_shards,\n multisearch_batches_total,\n            multisearch_partial_failures_total,\n            multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total,\n            vector_search_over_fetched_total,\n            vector_merge_strategy,\n            vector_embedder_drift_total,\n            cdc_events_published_total,\n            cdc_lag_seconds,\n            cdc_buffer_bytes,\n            cdc_dropped_total,\n            cdc_events_suppressed_total,\n            ttl_documents_expired_total,\n            ttl_sweep_duration_seconds,\n            ttl_pending_estimate,\n            tenant_queries_total,\n            tenant_pinned_groups,\n            tenant_fallback_total,\n            shadow_diff_total,\n            shadow_kendall_tau,\n            shadow_latency_delta_seconds,\n            shadow_errors_total,\n            rollover_events_total,\n            rollover_active_indexes,\n            rollover_documents_expired_total,\n            rollover_last_action_seconds,\n            canary_runs_total,\n            canary_latency_ms,\n            canary_assertion_failures_total,\n            admin_ui_sessions_total,\n            admin_ui_action_total,\n            admin_ui_destructive_action_total,\n            explain_requests_total,\n            explain_warnings_total,\n            explain_execute_total,\n            search_ui_sessions_total,\n            search_ui_queries_total,\n            search_ui_zero_hits_total,\n            search_ui_click_through_total,\n            search_ui_p95_ms,\n            admin_session_key_generated,\n            admin_session_revoked_total,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn encode_metrics(&self) -> Result<String, prometheus::Error> {\n        let encoder = TextEncoder::new();\n        let metric_families = self.registry.gather();\n        let mut buffer = Vec::new();\n        encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer)?;\n        Ok(String::from_utf8(buffer).map_err(|e| {\n            prometheus::Error::Msg(format!(\"failed to convert metrics to UTF-8: {}\", e))\n        })?)\n    }\n\n    pub fn admin_session_key_generated(&self) -> Gauge {\n        self.admin_session_key_generated.clone()\n    }\n\n    pub fn admin_session_revoked_total(&self) -> Counter {\n        self.admin_session_revoked_total.clone()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Generate a short request ID from UUIDv7.\n///\n/// UUIDv7 provides time-ordered unique IDs. We take the first 8 hex characters,\n/// hash them, and encode as hex for a short, URL-safe identifier.\npub fn generate_request_id() -> String {\n    let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n    let uuid_str = uuid.simple().to_string();\n    // Take first 8 chars (enough entropy for uniqueness)\n    let prefix = &uuid_str[..8];\n\n    // Hash to get a consistent short representation\n    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n    prefix.hash(&mut hasher);\n    let hash = hasher.finish();\n\n    // Encode as hex (16 chars = 64 bits)\n    format!(\"{:016x}\", hash)\n}\n\n/// Extension trait to add request ID extraction utilities.\npub trait RequestIdExt {\n    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String>;\n    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str);\n}\n\nimpl RequestIdExt for HeaderMap {\n    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String> {\n        self.get(\"x-request-id\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n    }\n\n    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str) {\n        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(id) {\n            self.insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Guard that decrements the in-flight gauge when dropped.\n///\n/// This ensures that even if the handler panics, the in-flight count\n/// is accurately decremented.\nstruct InFlightGuard {\n    metrics: Metrics,\n}\n\nimpl InFlightGuard {\n    fn new(metrics: Metrics) -> Self {\n        metrics.requests_in_flight.inc();\n        tracing::trace!(requests_in_flight = metrics.requests_in_flight.get(), \"request started\");\n        Self { metrics }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Drop for InFlightGuard {\n    fn drop(&mut self) {\n        self.metrics.requests_in_flight.dec();\n        tracing::trace!(requests_in_flight = self.metrics.requests_in_flight.get(), \"request completed\");\n    }\n}\n\n/// Extract the path template from the matched route.\n///\n/// Axum's MatchedPath extractor provides the route template (e.g., \"/indexes/{uid}/search\")\n/// instead of the actual path (e.g., \"/indexes/products/search\"), avoiding high-cardinality labels.\nfn extract_path_template(request: &Request) -> String {\n    request\n        .extensions()\n        .get::<axum::extract::MatchedPath>()\n        .map(|mp| mp.as_str())\n        .unwrap_or_else(|| request.uri().path())\n        .to_string()\n}\n\n/// Main middleware that combines request ID injection, structured logging, and Prometheus metrics.\npub async fn telemetry_middleware(\n    State(telemetry): State<TelemetryState>,\n    mut req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n    let method = req.method().clone();\n    let path_template = extract_path_template(&req);\n    let metrics = telemetry.metrics.clone();\n    let pod_id = telemetry.pod_id.clone();\n\n    // Generate or extract request ID\n    let request_id = req\n        .headers()\n        .get_request_id()\n        .unwrap_or_else(generate_request_id);\n    req.headers_mut().set_request_id(&request_id);\n\n    // Create span for structured logging with pod_id included\n    let span = info_span!(\n        \"request\",\n        request_id = %request_id,\n        pod_id = %pod_id,\n        method = %method,\n        path_template = %path_template,\n        path = %req.uri().path(),\n    );\n\n    let _guard = span.enter();\n\n    // Track in-flight requests\n    let in_flight = InFlightGuard::new(metrics.clone());\n\n    let response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    drop(in_flight);\n\n    let status = response.status();\n    let status_u16 = status.as_u16();\n    let duration = start.elapsed();\n\n    // Record Prometheus metrics\n    metrics\n        .request_duration\n        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n        .observe(duration.as_secs_f64());\n    metrics\n        .requests_total\n        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n        .inc();\n\n    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n    // Base fields: timestamp (from tracing-subscriber), level, message, duration_ms\n    // Additional fields (index, node_count, estimated_hits, degraded)\n    // are added by request handlers via the tracing span.\n    let message = format!(\"{} {}\", method, status);\n    if status.is_server_error() {\n        tracing::error!(\n            target: \"miroir.request\",\n            pod_id = %pod_id,\n            request_id = %request_id,\n            message = %message,\n            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n            status = status_u16,\n            method = %method,\n            path_template = %path_template,\n        );\n    } else if status.is_client_error() {\n        tracing::warn!(\n            target: \"miroir.request\",\n            pod_id = %pod_id,\n            request_id = %request_id,\n            message = %message,\n            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n            status = status_u16,\n            method = %method,\n            path_template = %path_template,\n        );\n    } else {\n        tracing::info!(\n            target: \"miroir.request\",\n            pod_id = %pod_id,\n            request_id = %request_id,\n            message = %message,\n            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n            status = status_u16,\n            method = %method,\n            path_template = %path_template,\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Ensure request ID is in response headers\n    let mut response = response;\n    if !response.headers().contains_key(\"x-request-id\") {\n        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(&request_id) {\n            response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n        }\n    }\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Create the metrics router for the :9090 server.\npub fn metrics_router() -> Router<Metrics> {\n    Router::new().route(\"/metrics\", get(metrics_handler))\n}\n\n/// Handler that returns Prometheus metrics in text exposition format.\nasync fn metrics_handler(State(metrics): State<Metrics>) -> String {\n    match metrics.encode_metrics() {\n        Ok(metrics) => metrics,\n        Err(e) => {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n            format!(\"# ERROR: failed to encode metrics: {}\\n\", e)\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Accessor methods for metrics that can be used by other parts of the application.\nimpl Metrics {\n    // ── Scatter-gather ──\n\n    pub fn record_scatter_fan_out(&self, size: u64) {\n        self.scatter_fan_out_size.observe(size as f64);\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_scatter_partial_responses(&self) {\n        self.scatter_partial_responses.inc();\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_scatter_retries(&self) {\n        self.scatter_retries.inc();\n    }\n\n    // ── Node health ──\n\n    pub fn set_node_healthy(&self, node_id: &str, healthy: bool) {\n        self.node_healthy.with_label_values(&[node_id]).set(if healthy { 1.0 } else { 0.0 });\n    }\n\n    pub fn record_node_request_duration(&self, node_id: &str, operation: &str, duration_secs: f64) {\n        self.node_request_duration.with_label_values(&[node_id, operation]).observe(duration_secs);\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_node_errors(&self, node_id: &str, error_type: &str) {\n        self.node_errors.with_label_values(&[node_id, error_type]).inc();\n    }\n\n    // ── Shards ──\n\n    pub fn set_shard_coverage(&self, coverage: f64) {\n        self.shard_coverage.set(coverage);\n    }\n\n    pub fn set_degraded_shards(&self, count: f64) {\n        self.degraded_shards.set(count);\n    }\n\n    pub fn set_shard_distribution(&self, node_id: &str, count: f64) {\n        self.shard_distribution.with_label_values(&[node_id]).set(count);\n    }\n\n    // ── Tasks ──\n\n    pub fn observe_task_processing_age(&self, age_secs: f64) {\n        self.task_processing_age.observe(age_secs);\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_tasks_total(&self, status: &str) {\n        self.tasks_total.with_label_values(&[status]).inc();\n    }\n\n    pub fn set_task_registry_size(&self, size: f64) {\n        self.task_registry_size.set(size);\n    }\n\n    // ── Rebalancer ──\n\n    pub fn set_rebalance_in_progress(&self, v: bool) {\n    }\n\n    pub fn observe_rebalance_duration(&self, secs: f64) {\n        self.rebalance_duration.observe(secs);\n    }\n\n    // ── §13.11 Multi-search ──\n\n    pub fn    fn test_request_id_generation() {\n        // Generate multiple IDs to verify format\n        for _ in 0..10 {\n            let id = generate_request_id();\n\n            // IDs should be 16 hex chars (64-bit hash)\n            assert_eq!(id.len(), 16);\n\n            // IDs should be hexadecimal\n            assert!(id.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()));\n        }\n\n        // Test that different UUID prefixes produce different IDs\n        let id1 = generate_request_id();\n        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(5));\n        let id2 = generate_request_id();\n        // In production, time ensures uniqueness; test just verifies format above\n        assert_eq!(id1.len(), 16);\n        assert_eq!(id2.len(), 16);\n    }\n\n \"/test\", \"200\"]).inc();\n        metrics.requests_in_flight.inc();\n        metrics.node_healthy.with_label_values(&[\"test-node\"]).set(1.0);\n MiroirCode};\n18\tuse miroir_core::config::Config;\n19\tuse miroir_core::scatter::{PreflightRequest, PreflightResponse, TermStats};\n20\tuse miroir_core::topology::Topology;\n21\tuse reqwest::Client;\n22\tuse serde_json::Value;\n23\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n24\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n25\t\n26\tuse crate::routes::{admin_endpoints::AppState, documents};\n27\t\n28\t/// Node client for communicating with Meilisearch.\n29\tpub struct MeilisearchClient {\n30\t    client: Client,\n31\t    master_key: String,\n32\t}\n33\t\n34\timpl MeilisearchClient {\n35\t    pub fn new(master_key: String) -> Self {\n36\t        let client = Client::builder()\n37\t            .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_millis(10000))\n38\t            .build()\n39\t            .expect(\"Failed to create HTTP client\");\n40\t\n41\t        Self { client, master_key }\n42\t    }\n43\t\n44\t    fn auth_header(&self) -> (&str, String) {\n45\t        (\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n46\t    }\n47\t\n48\t    /// POST to a node — generic broadcast helper.\n49\t    pub async fn post_raw(\n50\t        &self,\n51\t        address: &str,\n52\t        path: &str,\n53\t        body: &Value,\n54\t    ) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n55\t        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), path);\n56\t        let resp = self\n57\t            .client\n58\t            .post(&url)\n59\t            .header(self.auth_header().0, &self.auth_header().1)\n60\t            .json(body)\n61\t            .send()\n62\t            .await\n63\t            .map_err(|e| format!(\"request failed: {}\", e))?;\n64\t        let status = resp.status().as_u16();\n65\t        let text = resp.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"read body: {}\", e))?;\n66\t        Ok((status, text))\n67\t    }\n68\t\n69\t    /// PATCH to a node — generic broadcast helper.\n70\t    pub async fn patch_raw(\n71\t        &self,\n72\t        address: &str,\n73\t        path: &str,\n74\t        body: &Value,\n75\t    ) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n76\t        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), path);\n77\t        let resp = self\n78\t            .client\n79\t            .patch(&url)\n80\t            .header(self.auth_header().0, &self.auth_header().1)\n81\t            .json(body)\n82\t            .send()\n83\t            .await\n84\t            .map_err(|e| format!(\"request failed: {}\", e))?;\n85\t        let status = resp.status().as_u16();\n86\t        let text = resp.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"read body: {}\", e))?;\n87\t        Ok((status, text))\n88\t    }\n89\t\n90\t    /// DELETE on a node — generic helper.\n91\t    pub async fn delete_raw(\n92\t        &self,\n93\t        address: &str,\n94\t        path: &str,\n95\t    ) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n96\t        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), path);\n97\t        let resp = self\n98\t            .client\n99\t            .delete(&url)\n100\t            .header(self.auth_header().0, &self.auth_header().1)\n101\t            .send()\n102\t            .await\n103\t            .map_err(|e| format!(\"request failed: {}\", e))?;\n104\t        let status = resp.status().as_u16();\n105\t        let text = resp.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"read body: {}\", e))?;\n106\t        Ok((status, text))\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    /// GET from a node — generic helper.\n110\t    pub async fn get_raw(\n111\t        &self,\n112\t        address: &str,\n113\t        path: &str,\n114\t    ) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n115\t        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), path);\n116\t        let resp = self\n117\t            .client\n118\t            .get(&url)\n119\t            .header(self.auth_header().0, &self.auth_header().1)\n120\t            .send()\n121\t            .await\n122\t            .map_err(|e| format!(\"request failed: {}\", e))?;\n123\t        let status = resp.status().as_u16();\n124\t        let text = resp.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"read body: {}\", e))?;\n125\t        Ok((status, text))\n126\t    }\n127\t\n128\t    /// Get index statistics from Meilisearch.\n129\t    pub async fn get_index_stats(\n130\t        &self,\n131\t        address: &str,\n132\t        index_uid: &str,\n133\t    ) -> Result<Value, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n134\t        let url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/stats\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), index_uid);\n135\t        let response = self\n136\t            .client\n137\t            .get(&url)\n138\t            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n139\t            .send()\n140\t            .await?;\n141\t\n142\t        if !response.status().is_success() {\n143\t            return Err(format!(\"Failed to get stats: {}\", response.status()).into());\n144\t        }\n145\t\n146\t        response.json().await.map_err(|e| e.into())\n147\t    }\n148\t\n149\t    /// Get document frequency for a single term by searching.\n150\t    pub async fn get_term_df(\n151\t        &self,\n152\t        address: &str,\n153\t        index_uid: &str,\n154\t        term: &str,\n155\t        filter: &Option<Value>,\n156\t    ) -> Result<u64, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n157\t        let url = format!(\n158\t            \"{}/indexes/{}/search\",\n159\t            address.trim_end_matches('/'),\n160\t            index_uid\n161\t        );\n162\t\n163\t        let mut body = serde_json::json!({\n164\t            \"q\": term,\n165\t            \"limit\": 0,\n166\t        });\n167\t\n168\t        if let Some(f) = filter {\n169\t            body[\"filter\"] = f.clone();\n170\t        }\n171\t\n172\t        let response = self\n173\t            .client\n174\t            .post(&url)\n175\t            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n176\t            .json(&body)\n177\t            .send()\n178\t            .await?;\n179\t\n180\t        if !response.status().is_success() {\n181\t            return Err(format!(\"DF lookup failed: HTTP {}\", response.status()).into());\n182\t        }\n183\t\n184\t        let json: Value = response.json().await?;\n185\t        json.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\")\n186\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n187\t            .ok_or_else(|| \"Failed to parse estimatedTotalHits\".into())\n188\t    }\n189\t\n190\t    /// Estimate average document length by sampling a few documents.\n191\t    pub async fn estimate_avg_doc_length(\n192\t        &self,\n193\t        address: &str,\n194\t        index_uid: &str,\n195\t    ) -> Result<f64, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n196\t        let url = format!(\n197\t            \"{}/indexes/{}/documents\",\n198\t            address.trim_end_matches('/'),\n199\t            index_uid\n200\t        );\n201\t\n202\t        let response = self\n203\t            .client\n204\t            .get(&url)\n205\t            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n206\t            .query(&[(\"limit\", \"10\")])\n207\t            .send()\n208\t            .await?;\n209\t\n210\t        if !response.status().is_success() {\n211\t            return Ok(500.0);\n212\t        }\n213\t\n214\t        let json: Value = response.json().await?;\n215\t        let results = json.get(\"results\").and_then(|v| v.as_array());\n216\t\n217\t        if let Some(docs) = results {\n218\t            if docs.is_empty() {\n219\t                return Ok(500.0);\n220\t            }\n221\t\n222\t            let mut total_length = 0u64;\n223\t            let mut field_count = 0u64;\n224\t\n225\t            for doc in docs {\n226\t                if let Some(obj) = doc.as_object() {\n227\t                    for (_key, value) in obj {\n228\t                        if let Some(s) = value.as_str() {\n229\t                            total_length += s.len() as u64;\n230\t                            field_count += 1;\n231\t                        }\n232\t                    }\n233\t                }\n234\t            }\n235\t\n236\t            if field_count > 0 {\n237\t                return Ok(total_length as f64 / field_count as f64);\n238\t            }\n239\t        }\n240\t\n241\t        Ok(500.0)\n242\t    }\n243\t}\n244\t\n245\t/// Collect all healthy node addresses from config.\n246\tfn all_node_addresses(config: &Config) -> Vec<String> {\n247\t    config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect()\n248\t}\n249\t\n250\tpub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\n251\twhere\n252\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n253\t{\n254\t    Router::new()\n255\t        .route(\"/\", post(create_index_handler).get(list_indexes_handler))\n256\t        .route(\n257\t            \"/:index\",\n258\t            get(get_index_handler)\n259\t                .patch(update_index_handler)\n260\t                .delete(delete_index_handler),\n261\t        )\n262\t        .route(\"/:index/stats\", get(get_index_stats_handler))\n263\t        .route(\n264\t            \"/:index/settings\",\n265\t            get(get_settings_handler).patch(update_settings_handler),\n266\t        )\n267\t        .route(\n268\t            \"/:index/settings/*subpath\",\n269\t            get(get_settings_subpath_handler).patch(update_settings_subpath_handler),\n270\t        )\n271\t        .route(\"/:index/_preflight\", post(preflight_handler))\n272\t        .nest(\"/:index/documents\", documents::router::<S>())\n273\t}\n274\t\n275\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n276\t// POST /indexes — create index (broadcast + _miroir_shard)\n277\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n278\t\n279\tasync fn create_index_handler(\n280\t    Extension(_state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n281\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n282\t    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n283\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n284\t    let uid = body\n285\t        .get(\"uid\")\n286\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n287\t        .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(\n288\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n289\t            \"index creation requires a `uid` field\",\n290\t        ))?;\n291\t\n292\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n293\t    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n294\t    let mut created_on: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n295\t    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n296\t\n297\t    // Phase 1: Create index on every node sequentially\n298\t    for address in &nodes {\n299\t        match client.post_raw(address, \"/indexes\", &body).await {\n300\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n301\t                if first_response.is_none() {\n302\t                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n303\t                }\n304\t                created_on.push(address.clone());\n305\t            }\n306\t            Ok((status, text)) => {\n307\t                // Rollback: delete index on all previously created nodes\n308\t                rollback_delete_index(&client, uid, &created_on).await;\n309\t                let msg = format!(\n310\t                    \"index creation failed on node {}: HTTP {} — {}\",\n311\t                    address, status, text\n312\t                );\n313\t                return Err(forward_or_miroir(status, &text, &msg));\n314\t            }\n315\t            Err(e) => {\n316\t                rollback_delete_index(&client, uid, &created_on).await;\n317\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n318\t                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n319\t                    format!(\"index creation failed on node {}: {}\", address, e),\n320\t                ));\n321\t            }\n322\t        }\n323\t    }\n324\t\n325\t    // Phase 2: Add `_miroir_shard` to filterableAttributes on every node.\n326\t    // Read current filterableAttributes from first node, merge `_miroir_shard`,\n327\t    // then broadcast the merged list to all nodes.\n328\t    let mut merged_attrs: Vec<Value> = vec![serde_json::json!(\"_miroir_shard\")];\n329\t\n330\t    if let Some(first_addr) = nodes.first() {\n331\t        match client.get_raw(first_addr, &format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings\", uid)).await {\n332\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n333\t                if let Ok(settings) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&text) {\n334\t                    if let Some(existing) = settings.get(\"filterableAttributes\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n335\t                        for attr in existing {\n336\t                            let attr_str = attr.as_str().unwrap_or(\"\");\n337\t                            if attr_str != \"_miroir_shard\" && !attr_str.is_empty() {\n338\t                                merged_attrs.push(attr.clone());\n339\t                            }\n340\t                        }\n341\t                    }\n342\t                }\n343\t            }\n344\t            _ => {}\n345\t        }\n346\t    }\n347\t\n348\t    let filterable_patch = serde_json::json!({\n349\t        \"filterableAttributes\": merged_attrs\n350\t    });\n351\t\n352\t    let mut patch_ok: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n353\t    for address in &nodes {\n354\t        let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings\", uid);\n355\t        match client.patch_raw(address, &path, &filterable_patch).await {\n356\t            Ok((_status, _text)) if _status >= 200 && _status < 300 => {\n357\t                patch_ok.push(address.clone());\n358\t            }\n359\t            Ok((status, _text)) => {\n360\t                tracing::warn!(\n361\t                    node = %address,\n362\t                    status,\n363\t                    \"failed to set _miroir_shard filterable\"\n364\t                );\n365\t            }\n366\t            Err(e) => {\n367\t                tracing::warn!(\n368\t                    node = %address,\n369\t                    error = %e,\n370\t                    \"failed to set _miroir_shard filterable\"\n371\t                );\n372\t            }\n373\t        }\n374\t    }\n375\t\n376\t    if patch_ok.len() != nodes.len() {\n377\t        tracing::warn!(\n378\t            created = patch_ok.len(),\n379\t            total = nodes.len(),\n380\t            \"_miroir_shard filterableAttributes not set on all nodes\"\n381\t        );\n382\t    }\n383\t\n384\t    tracing::info!(\n385\t        index_uid = uid,\n386\t        nodes = nodes.len(),\n387\t        \"index created on all nodes\"\n388\t    );\n389\t\n390\t    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"uid\": uid, \"status\": \"created\"}))))\n391\t}\n392\t\n393\tasync fn rollback_delete_index(client: &MeilisearchClient, uid: &str, nodes: &[String]) {\n394\t    for address in nodes {\n395\t        let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}\", uid);\n396\t        match client.delete_raw(address, &path).await {\n397\t            Ok(_) => tracing::info!(node = %address, \"rollback: deleted index\"),\n398\t            Err(e) => tracing::error!(node = %address, error = %e, \"rollback: failed to delete index\"),\n399\t        }\n400\t    }\n401\t}\n402\t\n403\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n404\t// GET /indexes — list indexes (proxy to first node)\n405\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n406\t\n407\tasync fn list_indexes_handler(\n408\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n409\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, StatusCode> {\n410\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n411\t    let address = config.nodes.first().ok_or(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)?;\n412\t    let (status, text) = client.get_raw(&address.address, \"/indexes\").await.map_err(|e| {\n413\t        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"list indexes failed\");\n414\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n415\t    })?;\n416\t    if status >= 200 && status < 300 {\n417\t        let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"results\": []}));\n418\t        Ok(Json(json))\n419\t    } else {\n420\t        Err(StatusCode::from_u16(status).unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR))\n421\t    }\n422\t}\n423\t\n424\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n425\t// GET /indexes/{uid} — get single index (proxy)\n426\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n427\t\n428\tasync fn get_index_handler(\n429\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n430\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n431\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, StatusCode> {\n432\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n433\t    let address = config.nodes.first().ok_or(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)?;\n434\t    let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}\", index);\n435\t    let (status, text) = client.get_raw(&address.address, &path).await.map_err(|e| {\n436\t        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"get index failed\");\n437\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n438\t    })?;\n439\t    if status >= 200 && status < 300 {\n440\t        Ok(Json(serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null)))\n441\t    } else {\n442\t        Err(StatusCode::from_u16(status).unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR))\n443\t    }\n444\t}\n445\t\n446\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n447\t// PATCH /indexes/{uid} — update index metadata (broadcast with rollback)\n448\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n449\t\n450\tasync fn update_index_handler(\n451\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n452\t    Extension(_state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n453\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n454\t    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n455\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n456\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n457\t    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n458\t    let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}\", index);\n459\t\n460\t    // Snapshot current index state from all nodes before applying changes\n461\t    let mut snapshots: Vec<(String, Value)> = Vec::new();\n462\t    for address in &nodes {\n463\t        match client.get_raw(address, &path).await {\n464\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n465\t                let snapshot: Value = serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n466\t                snapshots.push((address.clone(), snapshot));\n467\t            }\n468\t            Ok((status, text)) => {\n469\t                return Err(forward_or_miroir(\n470\t                    status,\n471\t                    &text,\n472\t                    &format!(\"failed to snapshot index on {}: HTTP {}\", address, status),\n473\t                ));\n474\t            }\n475\t            Err(e) => {\n476\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n477\t                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n478\t                    format!(\"failed to snapshot index on {}: {}\", address, e),\n479\t                ));\n480\t            }\n481\t        }\n482\t    }\n483\t\n484\t    // Apply update sequentially to each node\n485\t    let mut applied: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n486\t    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n487\t\n488\t    for (address, _) in &snapshots {\n489\t        match client.patch_raw(address, &path, &body).await {\n490\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n491\t                if first_response.is_none() {\n492\t                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n493\t                }\n494\t                applied.push(address.clone());\n495\t            }\n496\t            Ok((status, text)) => {\n497\t                rollback_index_update(&client, &path, &snapshots, &applied).await;\n498\t                let msg = format!(\n499\t                    \"index update failed on {}: HTTP {} — {}\",\n500\t                    address, status, text\n501\t                );\n502\t                return Err(forward_or_miroir(status, &text, &msg));\n503\t            }\n504\t            Err(e) => {\n505\t                rollback_index_update(&client, &path, &snapshots, &applied).await;\n506\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n507\t                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n508\t                    format!(\"index update failed on {}: {}\", address, e),\n509\t                ));\n510\t            }\n511\t        }\n512\t    }\n513\t\n514\t    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"uid\": index, \"status\": \"updated\"}))))\n515\t}\n516\t\n517\t/// Rollback index metadata updates by restoring pre-change snapshots.\n518\tasync fn rollback_index_update(\n519\t    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n520\t    path: &str,\n521\t    snapshots: &[(String, Value)],\n522\t    applied: &[String],\n523\t) {\n524\t    for address in applied {\n525\t        if let Some((_, snapshot)) = snapshots.iter().find(|(a, _)| a == address) {\n526\t            match client.patch_raw(address, path, snapshot).await {\n527\t                Ok((_status, _text)) if _status >= 200 && _status < 300 => {\n528\t                    tracing::info!(node = %address, \"index update rollback succeeded\");\n529\t                }\n530\t                Ok((status, _text)) => {\n531\t                    tracing::error!(\n532\t                        node = %address,\n533\t                        status,\n534\t                        \"index update rollback failed\"\n535\t                    );\n536\t                }\n537\t                Err(e) => {\n538\t                    tracing::error!(node = %address, error = %e, \"index update rollback failed\");\n539\t                }\n540\t            }\n541\t        }\n542\t    }\n543\t}\n544\t\n545\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n546\t// DELETE /indexes/{uid} — broadcast delete\n547\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n548\t\n549\tasync fn delete_index_handler(\n550\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n551\t    Extension(_state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n552\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n553\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n554\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n555\t    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n556\t    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n557\t    let mut errors: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n558\t\n559\t    for address in &nodes {\n560\t        let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}\", index);\n561\t        match client.delete_raw(address, &path).await {\n562\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n563\t                if first_response.is_none() {\n564\t                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n565\t                }\n566\t            }\n567\t            Ok((status, text)) => {\n568\t                errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {} — {}\", address, status, text));\n569\t            }\n570\t            Err(e) => {\n571\t                errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", address, e));\n572\t            }\n573\t        }\n574\t    }\n575\t\n576\t    if !errors.is_empty() && first_response.is_none() {\n577\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n578\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n579\t            format!(\"index deletion failed on all nodes: {}\", errors.join(\"; \")),\n580\t        ));\n581\t    }\n582\t\n583\t    if !errors.is_empty() {\n584\t        tracing::warn!(\n585\t            index_uid = %index,\n586\t            errors = errors.len(),\n587\t            \"index deletion partially failed\"\n588\t        );\n589\t    }\n590\t\n591\t    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"taskUid\": 0, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}))))\n592\t}\n593\t\n594\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n595\t// GET /indexes/{uid}/stats — fan out, aggregate\n596\t// }\n623\t                }\n624\t            }\n625\t            Err(e) => {\n626\t                tracing::warn!(node = %address, error = %e, \"stats fan-out failed\");\n627\t            }\n628\t Apply update sequentially\n156\t    let mut applied: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n157\t    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n158\t\n159\t    for (address, _snapshot) in &snapshots {\n160\t        match client.patch_raw(address, &path, &body).await {\n161\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n162\t                if first_response.is_none() {\n163\t                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n164\t                }\n165\t                applied.push(address.clone());\n166\t            }\n167\t            Ok((status, text)) => {\n168\t                rollback_key_update(&client, &path, &snapshots, &applied).await;\n169\t                let msg = format!(\n170\t                    \"key update failed on {}: HTTP {} — {}\",\n171\t                    address, status, text\n172\t                );\n173\t                return Err(forward_or_miroir(status, &text, &msg));\n174\t            }\n175\t            Err(e) => {\n176\t                rollback_key_update(&client, &path, &snapshots, &applied).await;\n177\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n178\t                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n179\t                    format!(\"key update failed on {}: {}\", address, e),\n180\t                ));\n181\t            }\n182\t        }\n183\t    }\n184\t\n185\t    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"status\": \"updated\"}))))\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\t/// Rollback key updates by restoring pre-change snapshots.\n189\tasync fn rollback_key_update(\n190\t    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n191\t    path: &str,\n192\t    snapshots: &[(String, Value)],\n193\t    applied: &[String],\n194\t) {\n195\t    for address in applied {\n196\t        if let Some((_, snapshot)) = snapshots.iter().find(|(a, _)| a == address) {\n197\t            match client.patch_raw(address, path, snapshot).await {\n198\t                Ok((_status, _text)) if _status >= 200 && _status < 300 => {\n199\t                    tracing::info!(node = %address, \"key rollback succeeded\");\n200\t                }\n201\t                Ok((status, _text)) => {\n202\t                    tracing::error!(node = %address, status, \"key rollback failed\");\n203\t                }\n204\t                Err(e) => {\n205\t                    tracing::error!(node = %address, error = %e, \"key rollback failed\");\n206\t                }\n207\t            }\n208\t        }\n209\t    }\n210\t}\n211\t\n212\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n213\t// DELETE /keys/{key} — delete key (all-or-nothing broadcast)\n214\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n215\t\n216\tasync fn delete_key_handler(\n217\t    Path(key): Path<String>,\n218\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n219\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n220\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n221\t    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n222\t    let path = format!(\"/keys/{}\", key);\n223\t    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n224\t    let mut errors: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n225\t\n226\t    for address in &nodes {\n227\t        match client.delete_raw(address, &path).await {\n228\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n229\t                if first_response.is_none() {\n230\t                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n231\t                }\n232\t            }\n233\t            Ok((status, text)) => {\n234\t                errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {} — {}\", address, status, text));\n235\t            }\n236\t            Err(e) => {\n237\t                errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", address, e));\n238\t            }\n239\t        }\n240\t    }\n241\t\n242\t    if !errors.is_empty() && first_response.is_none() {\n243\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n244\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n245\t            format!(\"key deletion failed on all nodes: {}\", errors.join(\"; \")),\n246\t        ));\n247\t    }\n248\t\n249\t    if !errors.is_empty() {\n250\t        // Hash the key identifier for correlation without logging the raw value (plan §10: no PII).\n251\t        let key_hash = {\n252\t            use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n253\t            let mut h = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n254\t            key.hash(&mut h);\n255\t            format!(\"{:016x}\", h.finish())\n256\t        };\n257\t        tracing::warn!(key_hash = %key_hash, errors = errors.len(), \"key deletion partially failed\");\n258\t    }\n259\t\n260\t    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"status\": \"deleted\"}))))\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n264\t// GET /keys — list keys (proxy to first node)\n265\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n266\t\n267\tasync fn list_keys_handler(\n268\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n269\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, StatusCode> {\n270\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n271\t    let address = config.nodes.first().ok_or(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)?;\n272\t    let (status, text) = client.get_raw(&address.address, \"/keys\").await.map_err(|e| {\n273\t        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"list keys failed\");\n274\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n275\t    })?;\n276\t    if status >= 200 && status < 300 {\n277\t        Ok(Json(serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null)))\n278\t    } else {\n279\t        Err(StatusCode::from_u16(status).unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR))\n280\t    }\n281\t}\n282\t\n283\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n284\t// router for task endpoints.\n92\tpub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\n93\twhere\n94\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n95\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n96\t{\n97\t    Router::new()\n98\t Query<TasksQuery>,\n106\t    State(state): State<S>,\n107\t) -> Result<Json<TasksListResponse>, StatusCode>\n108\twhere\n109\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n110\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n111\t{\n112\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n113\t\n114\t    // Parse status filter (supports comma-separated values, takes first)\n115\t    let status_filter = query.statuses.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n116\t        s.split(',')\n117\t            .next()\n118\t            .and_then(|status_str| match status_str.trim() {\n119\t                \"succeeded\" | \"Succeeded\" => Some(TaskStatus::Succeeded),\n120\t                \"failed\" | \"Failed\" => Some(TaskStatus::Failed),\n121\t                \"processing\" | \"Processing\" => Some(TaskStatus::Processing),\n122\t                \"enqueued\" | \"Enqueued\" => Some(TaskStatus::Enqueued),\n123\t                \"canceled\" | \"Canceled\" => Some(TaskStatus::Canceled),\n124\t                _ => None,\n125\t            })\n126\t    });\n127\t\n128\t    // Parse indexUids filter (supports comma-separated values, takes first)\n129\t    let index_uid_filter = query.indexUids.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n130\t        s.split(',')\n131\t            .next()\n132\t            .map(|uid| uid.trim().to_string())\n133\t    });\n134\t\n135\t    // Parse types filter ty.trim().to_string())\n140\t    });\n141\t\n142\t    // Build filter with all parameters\n143\t    let filter = miroir_core::task::TaskFilter {\n144\t        status: status_filter,\n145\t // List tasks from registry\n153\t    let tasks = state\n154\t        .task_registry\n155\t        .list(filter)\n156\t        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n157\t\n158\t    // Get response\n165\t    let results = tasks.into_iter().map(task_to_response).collect();\n166\t\n167\t    let limit = query.limit.unwrap_or(20);\n168\t    let from = query.from.unwrap_or(0);\n169\t\n170\t    Ok(Json(TasksListResponse {\n171\t        results,\n172\t        limit,\n173\t        from,\n174\t        total,\n175\t    }))\n176\t}\n177\t\n178\t/// GET /tasks/{id} — Get a specific task by Miroir task ID.\n179\t///\n180\t/// Polls all mapped nodes for their current task status and aggregates the result.\n181\tasync fn get_task<S>(\n182\t    Path(id): Path<String>,\n183\t    State(state): State<S>,\n184\t) -> Result<Json<TaskResponse>, StatusCode>\n185\twhere\n186\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n187\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n188\t{\n189\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n190\t\n191\t    // Validate task ID format\n192\t    if !id.starts_with(\"mtask-\") {\n193\t        return Err(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST);\n194\t    }\n195\t\n196\t    let mut task = state\n197\t        .task_registry\n198\t        .get(&id)\n199\t        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n200\t        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n201\t\n202\t    // Poll nodes for current status if task is not terminal\n203\t    if !matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n204\t        let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n205\t        let client = HttpClient::new(\n206\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n207\t            state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n208\t        );\n209\t\n210\t        // Update node task statuses by polling each node\n211\t        let mut node_errors = HashMap::new();\n212\t        let mut any_processing = false;\n213\t        let mut all_succeeded = true;\n214\t        let mut any_failed = false;\n215\t\n216\t        for (node_id_str, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n217\t            let node_id = miroir_core::topology::NodeId::new(node_id_str.clone());\n218\t\n219\t            // Skip polling if node task is already terminal\n220\t            if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed) {\n221\t                if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Failed) {\n222\t                    any_failed = true;\n223\t                    all_succeeded = false;\n224\t                }\n225\t                continue;\n226\t            }\n227\t\n228\t            // Get node address from topology\n229\t            let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n230\t                Some(n) => n,\n231\t                None => {\n232\t                    node_errors.insert(node_id_str.clone(), \"node not found in topology\".to_string());\n233\t                    any_failed = true;\n234\t                    all_succeeded = false;\n235\t                    continue;\n236\t                }\n237\t            };\n238\t\n239\t            // Poll this node for task status\n240\t            let req = TaskStatusRequest { task_uid: node_task.task_uid };\n241\t            match client.get_task_status(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n242\t                Ok(resp) => {\n243\t                    let new_status = resp.to_node_status();\n244\t                    // Update the node task status in the registry\n245\t                    let _ = state.task_registry.update_node_task(&id, node_id_str, new_status);\n246\t\n247\t                    // Track overall status\n248\t                    match new_status {\n249\t                        NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n250\t                        NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {\n251\t                            any_failed = true;\n252\t                            all_succeeded = false;\n253\t                            if let Some(error) = resp.error {\n254\t                                node_errors.insert(node_id_str.clone(), error);\n255\t                            }\n256\t                        }\n257\t                        NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n258\t                            any_processing = true;\n259\t                            all_succeeded = false;\n260\t                        }\n261\t                        NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued => {\n262\t                            all_succeeded = false;\n263\t                        }\n264\t                    }\n265\t                }\n266\t                Err(e) => {\n267\t                    tracing::warn!(node = %node_id_str, task = %id, error = ?e, \"failed to poll node for task\");\n268\t                    // Don't mark as failed on network error - may be transient\n269\t                    all_succeeded = false;\n270\t                }\n271\t            }\n272\t        }\n273\t\n274\t        // Update overall task status based on node task statuses\n275\t        let new_status = if any_failed {\n276\t            TaskStatus::Failed\n277\t        } else if all_succeeded {\n278\t            TaskStatus::Succeeded\n279\t        } else if any_processing {\n280\t            TaskStatus::Processing\n281\t        } else {\n282\t            TaskStatus::Enqueued\n283\t        };\n284\t\n285\t        // Update the task status in the registry\n286\t        let _ = state.task_registry.update_status(&id, new_status);\n287\t\n288\t        // Update the task with node errors and new status\n289\t        task.status = new_status;\n290\t        task.node_errors = node_errors;\n291\t\n292\t        // Set timestamps\n293\t        if matches!(new_status, TaskStatus::Processing) && task.started_at.is_none() {\n294\t            task.started_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n295\t                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n296\t                .unwrap_or_default()\n297\t                .as_millis() as u64);\n298\t        }\n299\t\n300\t        if matches!(new_status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) && task.finished_at.is_none() {\n301\t            task.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n302\t                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n303\t                .unwrap_or_default()\n304\t                .as_millis() as u64);\n305\t        }\n306\t    }\n307\t\n308\t    Ok(Json(task_to_response(task)))\n309\t}\n310\t\n311\t/// DELETE /tasks/{id} — Cancel a task (best-effort).\n312\tasync fn delete_task<S>(\n313\t    Path(id): Path<String>,\n314\t    State(state): State<S>,\n315\t) -> Result<Json<TaskResponse>, StatusCode>\n316\twhere\n317\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n318\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n319\t{\n320\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n321\t\n322\t    // Validate task ID format\n323\t    if !id.starts_with(\"mtask-\") {\n324\t        return Err(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST);\n325\t    }\n326\t\n327\t    // Get the task first\n328\t    let task = state\n329\t        .task_registry\n330\t        .get(&id)\n331\t        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n332\t        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n333\t\n334\t    // Update status to canceled if not already terminal\n335\t    if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Enqueued | TaskStatus::Processing) {\n336\t        state\n337\t            .task_registry\n338\t            .update_status(&id, TaskStatus::Canceled)\n339\t            .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n340\t    }\n341\t\n342\t    // Return the updated task\n343\t    let updated = state\n344\t        .task_registry\n345\t        .get(&id)\n346\t        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n347\t        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n348\t\n349\t    Ok(Json(task_to_response(updated)))\n350\t}\n351\t\n352\t/// Convert MiroirTask to Meilisearch-compatible TaskResponse.\n353\tfn task_to_response(task: MiroirTask) -> TaskResponse {\n354\t    let status_str = match task.status {\n355\t        TaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n356\t        TaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n357\t        TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n358\t        TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n359\t        TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n360\t    };\n361\t\n362\t    let enqueued_at = format_millis_timestamp(task.created_at);\n363\t    let started_at = task.started_at.map(|t| format_millis_timestamp(t));\n364\t    let finished_at = task.finished_at.map(|t| format_millis_timestamp(t));\n365\t\n366\t    let error = if task.status == TaskStatus::Failed {\n367\t        Some(TaskError {\n368\t            code: \"internal_error\".to_string(),\n369\t            message: task.error.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n370\t                if task.node_errors.is_empty() {\n371\t                    \"task failed\".to_string()\n372\t                } else {\n373\t                    format!(\"{} node(s) failed\", task.node_errors.len())\n374\t                }\n375\t            }),\n376\t            error_type: \"internal_error\".to_string(),\n377\t            details: task.node_errors.clone(),\n378\t        })\n379\t    } else {\n380\t        None\n381\t    };\n382\t\n383\t    // Build per-node details\n384\t    let mut nodes = HashMap::new();\n385\t    for (node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n386\t        let node_status = match node_task.status {\n387\t            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n388\t            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n389\t            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n390\t            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n391\t        };\n392\t        nodes.insert(\n393\t            node_id.clone(),\n394\t            NodeTaskDetail {\n395\t                task_uid: node_task.task_uid,\n396\t                status: node_status.to_string(),\n397\t            },\n398\t        );\n399\t    }\n400\t\n401\t    let details = Some(TaskDetails {\n402\t status_str.to_string(),\n410\t        task_type: task.task_type,\n411\t        details,\n412\t        error,\n413\t        duration: None,\n414\t        enqueuedAt: enqueued_at,\n415\t        startedAt: started_at,\n416\t        finishedAt: finished_at,\n417\t    }\n418\t}\n419\t\n420\t/// Format milliseconds since epoch as ISO 8601 timestamp.\n421\tfn format_millis_timestamp(millis: u64) -> String {\n422\t    // Simple ISO 8601 format without chrono dependency\n423\t    let secs = millis / 1000;\n424\t    let millis_part = millis % 1000;\n425\t\n426\t    // Calculate date components (simplified, assumes Unix epoch)\n427\t    // This is a rough approximation - for production use chrono or time crate\n428\t    let days_since_epoch = secs / 86400;\n429\t    let seconds_in_day = secs % 86400;\n430\t\n431\t    let hours = seconds_in_day / 3600;\n432\t    let minutes = (seconds_in_day % 3600) / 60;\n433\t    let seconds = seconds_in_day % 60;\n434\t\n435\t    // Days from 1970-01-01 to 2000-01-01 is roughly 10957 days\n436\t    // This is a very rough approximation for formatting\n437\t    format!(\n438\t        \"{:04}-{:02}-{:02}T{:02}:{:02}:{:02}.{:03}Z\",\n439\t        1970 + days_since_epoch / 365,\n440\t        1 + (days_since_epoch % 365) / 30,\n441\t        1 + (days_since_epoch % 30),\n442\t        hours,\n443\t        minutes,\n444\t        seconds,\n445\t        millis_part\n446\t    )\n447\t}\n448\t\n449\t#[cfg(test)]\n450\tmod tests {\n451\t    use super::*;\n452\t    use miroir_core::task::{NodeTask, NodeTaskStatus, TaskFilter};\n453\t    use
