{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_9c60b5141092492aa5a76659","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Bearer-token dispatch per plan §5 rules 0–5.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Three token types can appear on `Authorization: Bearer <value>` simultaneously:\n4\t//! the `master_key`, the `admin_key`, and a search UI JWT. Miroir resolves them\n5\t//! deterministically in the order specified by §5.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! JWT signing-secret rotation (plan §9):\n8\t//! - Primary secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET`) signs new tokens; `kid` header identifies it.\n9\t//! - Optional previous secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS`) is present only during\n10\t//!   the rotation overlap window. Validation accepts either secret.\n11\t\n12\tuse axum::{\n13\t    extract::{FromRef, Request, State},\n14\t    http::{HeaderMap, Method},\n15\t    middleware::Next,\n16\t    response::{IntoResponse, Response},\n17\t};\n18\tuse base64::{engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD, Engine as _};\n19\tuse dashmap::DashMap;\n20\tuse hmac::{Hmac, Mac};\n21\tuse miroir_core::{task_store::TaskStore, MeilisearchError, MiroirCode};\n22\tuse prometheus::Counter;\n23\tuse rand::RngCore;\n24\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n25\tuse sha2::Sha256;\n26\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n27\tuse subtle::ConstantTimeEq;\n28\t\n29\tuse crate::admin_session::{self, SealKey};\n30\t\n31\ttype HmacSha256 = Hmac<Sha256>;\n32\t\n33\t/// Extension carried in the request after successful cookie unseal.\n34\t/// Handlers extract this to look up the session in the task store.\n35\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n36\tpub struct AdminSessionId(pub String);\n37\t\n38\t/// State for CSRF middleware, combining AuthState with task store access.\n39\t#[derive(Clone)]\n40\tpub struct CsrfState {\n41\t    pub auth: AuthState,\n42\t    pub redis_store: Option<miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore>,\n43\t}\n44\t\n45\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n46\t// JWT claims (plan §13.21)\n47\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n48\t\n49\t/// Claims embedded in a search UI JWT session token.\n50\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n51\tpub struct JwtClaims {\n52\t    /// Subject — user identifier or \"anonymous\".\n53\t    pub sub: String,\n54\t    /// Index this token grants access to.\n55\t    pub idx: String,\n56\t    /// Granted scope (e.g. \"search\").\n57\t    pub scope: String,\n58\t    /// Issued-at timestamp (seconds since epoch).\n59\t    pub iat: u64,\n60\t    /// Expiration timestamp (seconds since epoch).\n61\t    pub exp: u64,\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\t/// Key ID embedded in the JWT header to identify which secret signed it.\n65\tconst KID_PRIMARY: &str = \"primary\";\n66\tconst KID_PREVIOUS: &str = \"previous\";\n67\t\n68\t/// JWT header (always HS256).\n69\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n70\tstruct JwtHeader {\n71\t    alg: String,\n72\t    kid: String,\n73\t    typ: String,\n74\t}\n75\t\n76\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n77\t// Minimal HS256 JWT encode / decode (no external JWT crate needed)\n78\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n79\t\n80\t/// Encode and sign a JWT with the given secret.\n81\tfn jwt_encode(header: &JwtHeader, claims: &JwtClaims, secret: &[u8]) -> Result<String, String> {\n82\t    let header_json = serde_json::to_string(header).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n83\t    let claims_json = serde_json::to_string(claims).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n84\t\n85\t    let header_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(header_json.as_bytes());\n86\t    let payload_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(claims_json.as_bytes());\n87\t\n88\t    let signing_input = format!(\"{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64);\n89\t\n90\t    let mut mac =\n91\t        HmacSha256::new_from_slice(secret).map_err(|e| format!(\"HMAC init: {}\", e))?;\n92\t    mac.update(signing_input.as_bytes());\n93\t    let sig = mac.finalize().into_bytes();\n94\t    let sig_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(sig);\n95\t\n96\t    Ok(format!(\"{}.{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64, sig_b64))\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\t/// Decode and verify a JWT with the given secret. Returns (header, claims).\n100\tfn jwt_decode(\n101\t    token: &str,\n102\t    secret: &[u8],\n103\t) -> Result<(JwtHeader, JwtClaims), JwtValidationError> {\n104\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n105\t    if parts.len() != 3 {\n106\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    let header_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n110\t        .decode(parts[0])\n111\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n112\t    let header: JwtHeader =\n113\t        serde_json::from_slice(&header_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n114\t\n115\t    if header.alg != \"HS256\" {\n116\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    // Verify signature\n120\t    let signing_input = format!(\"{}.{}\", parts[0], parts[1]);\n121\t    let mut mac = HmacSha256::new_from_slice(secret).unwrap();\n122\t    mac.update(signing_input.as_bytes());\n123\t    let expected_sig = mac.finalize().into_bytes();\n124\t\n125\t    let actual_sig = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n126\t        .decode(parts[2])\n127\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)?;\n128\t\n129\t    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n130\t    let sig_valid: bool = actual_sig.ct_eq(&expected_sig).into();\n131\t    if !sig_valid {\n132\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature);\n133\t    }\n134\t\n135\t    // Decode claims\n136\t    let claims_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n137\t        .decode(parts[1])\n138\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n139\t    let claims: JwtClaims =\n140\t        serde_json::from_slice(&claims_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n141\t\n142\t    // Check expiration with 30s leeway\n143\t    let now = epoch_seconds();\n144\t    if claims.exp + 30 < now {\n145\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired);\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    Ok((header, claims))\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n152\t// Auth state (shared via axum State)\n153\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n154\t\n155\t/// Configuration needed by the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n156\t#[derive(Clone)]\n157\tpub struct AuthState {\n158\t    pub master_key: String,\n159\t    pub admin_key: String,\n160\t    /// HMAC secret for signing/validating search UI JWTs (primary).\n161\t    pub jwt_primary: Option<String>,\n162\t    /// Optional previous secret active during rotation overlap window.\n163\t    pub jwt_previous: Option<String>,\n164\t    /// Key for sealing/unsealing admin session cookies (XChaCha20-Poly1305).\n165\t    pub seal_key: SealKey,\n166\t    /// In-memory set of revoked admin session IDs (populated on logout, Pub/Sub).\n167\t    pub revoked_sessions: Arc<DashMap<String, ()>>,\n168\t    /// Counter for revoked admin sessions (miroir_admin_session_revoked_total).\n169\t    pub admin_session_revoked_total: Counter,\n170\t}\n171\t\n172\timpl std::fmt::Debug for AuthState {\n173\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n174\t        f.debug_struct(\"AuthState\")\n175\t            .field(\"master_key\", &\"[redacted]\")\n176\t            .field(\"admin_key\", &\"[redacted]\")\n177\t            .field(\"jwt_primary\", &self.jwt_primary.as_ref().map(|_| \"[set]\"))\n178\t            .field(\"jwt_previous\", &self.jwt_previous.as_ref().map(|_| \"[set]\"))\n179\t            .field(\"seal_key\", &self.seal_key)\n180\t            .field(\"revoked_sessions\", &self.revoked_sessions.len())\n181\t            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n182\t    }\n183\t}\n184\t\n185\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n186\t// JWT signing / validation helpers\n187\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n188\t\n189\timpl AuthState {\n190\t    /// Create a new signed JWT session token for the given index.\n191\t    /// Always signs with the primary secret; `kid` header identifies it.\n192\t    pub fn sign_jwt(&self, sub: &str, idx: &str, scope: &str, ttl_s: u64) -> Option<String> {\n193\t        let secret = self.jwt_primary.as_ref()?;\n194\t        let now = epoch_seconds();\n195\t        let claims = JwtClaims {\n196\t            sub: sub.to_string(),\n197\t            idx: idx.to_string(),\n198\t            scope: scope.to_string(),\n199\t            iat: now,\n200\t            exp: now + ttl_s,\n201\t        };\n202\t        let header = JwtHeader {\n203\t            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n204\t            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n205\t            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n206\t        };\n207\t        jwt_encode(&header, &claims, secret.as_bytes()).ok()\n208\t    }\n209\t\n210\t    /// Validate a JWT string against either the primary or previous secret.\n211\t    /// Returns the parsed claims if validation succeeds, or an error.\n212\t    pub fn validate_jwt(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, JwtValidationError> {\n213\t        // Try primary secret first\n214\t        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_primary {\n215\t            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n216\t                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n217\t                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n218\t                _ => {} // signature mismatch — try previous\n219\t            }\n220\t        }\n221\t\n222\t        // Try previous secret (rotation overlap window)\n223\t        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_previous {\n224\t            if secret.is_empty() {\n225\t                return Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty);\n226\t            }\n227\t            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n228\t                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n229\t                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n230\t                _ => {} // signature mismatch\n231\t            }\n232\t        }\n233\t\n234\t        Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)\n235\t    }\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t/// Errors returned by JWT validation.\n239\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n240\tpub enum JwtValidationError {\n241\t    /// Token structure is invalid or alg is not HS256.\n242\t    Malformed,\n243\t    /// HMAC signature did not match any loaded secret.\n244\t    InvalidSignature,\n245\t    /// Token has expired.\n246\t    Expired,\n247\t    /// `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS` is set to the empty string (leak response).\n248\t    PreviousSecretEmpty,\n249\t}\n250\t\n251\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n252\t// CSRF token generation (plan §9)\n253\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n254\t\n255\t/// Generate a cryptographically random CSRF token.\n256\t/// Returns a URL-safe base64-encoded 32-byte token.\n257\tpub fn generate_csrf_token() -> String {\n258\t    let mut bytes = [0u8; 32];\n259\t    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n260\t    URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(&bytes)\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t/// Extract the CSRF token from the `X-CSRF-Token` header.\n264\tpub fn extract_csrf_token(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n265\t    headers.get(\"X-CSRF-Token\")?.to_str().ok().map(String::from)\n266\t}\n267\t\n268\t/// Constant-time comparison of CSRF tokens.\n269\tpub fn constant_time_csrf_compare(token: &str, expected: &str) -> bool {\n270\t    constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), expected.as_bytes())\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t/// Validate a CSRF token against the expected session token.\n274\t/// Returns Ok(()) if the token matches, or a CsrfMismatch error.\n275\tpub fn validate_csrf_token(provided: &str, expected: &str) -> Result<(), MiroirCode> {\n276\t    if constant_time_csrf_compare(provided, expected) {\n277\t        Ok(())\n278\t    } else {\n279\t        Err(MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch)\n280\t    }\n281\t}\n282\t\n283\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n284\t// Origin validation (plan §9)\n285\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n286\t\n287\t/// Result of origin validation.\n288\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n289\tpub enum OriginVerdict {\n290\t    /// Origin is allowed.\n291\t    Allowed,\n292\t    /// Origin is not in the allowed list.\n293\t    Forbidden,\n294\t    /// No Origin/Referer header present (for same-origin requests).\n295\t    Missing,\n296\t}\n297\t\n298\t/// Validate the Origin header against the allowed origins list.\n299\t/// Handles special \"same-origin\" value by comparing against the Host header.\n300\tpub fn validate_origin(\n301\t    headers: &HeaderMap,\n302\t    allowed_origins: &[String],\n303\t    is_same_origin_by_default: bool,\n304\t) -> OriginVerdict {\n305\t    // Try Origin header first (preferred for POST/DELETE/PUT)\n306\t    let origin = headers.get(\"origin\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok());\n307\t\n308\t    // Fall back to Referer header (for navigational requests)\n309\t    let referer = origin.or_else(|| headers.get(\"referer\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()));\n310\t\n311\t    let provided_origin = match referer {\n312\t        Some(o) => o,\n313\t        None => {\n314\t            // No Origin or Referer header - for same-origin requests, this is acceptable\n315\t            return if is_same_origin_by_default {\n316\t                OriginVerdict::Missing\n317\t            } else {\n318\t                OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n319\t            };\n320\t        }\n321\t    };\n322\t\n323\t    // Strip path from Referer to get origin\n324\t    let provided_origin = if let Some(ref_hdr) = headers.get(\"referer\") {\n325\t        if let Ok(ref_val) = ref_hdr.to_str() {\n326\t            // Find the first '/' after \"https://\" (skip the first 8 chars: \"https://\")\n327\t            if let Some(idx) = ref_val.chars().enumerate().skip(8).find(|(_, c)| *c == '/').map(|(i, _)| i) {\n328\t                &ref_val[..idx]\n329\t            } else {\n330\t                ref_val\n331\t            }\n332\t        } else {\n333\t            provided_origin\n334\t        }\n335\t    } else {\n336\t        provided_origin\n337\t    };\n338\t\n339\t    // Check against allowed origins\n340\t    for allowed in allowed_origins {\n341\t        // Special \"same-origin\" value - compare against Host header\n342\t        if allowed == \"same-origin\" {\n343\t            if let Some(host) = headers.get(\"host\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()) {\n344\t                // Construct origin from scheme (https) and host\n345\t                let same_origin = format!(\"https://{}\", host);\n346\t                if provided_origin == same_origin || provided_origin == host {\n347\t                    return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n348\t                }\n349\t            }\n350\t        } else if allowed == \"*\" {\n351\t            // Wildcard allows any origin\n352\t            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n353\t        } else if provided_origin == allowed {\n354\t            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n355\t        }\n356\t    }\n357\t\n358\t    OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n359\t}\n360\t\n361\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n362\t// CSP header builder (plan §9)\n363\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n364\t\n365\t/// Build a CSP header value by merging base template with overrides.\n366\t/// Overrides are merged additively - they never replace the base template.\n367\tpub fn build_csp_header(\n368\t    base_template: &str,\n369\t    overrides: &miroir_core::config::CspOverridesConfig,\n370\t) -> String {\n371\t    let mut directives: Vec<(String, Vec<String>)> = base_template\n372\t        .split(';')\n373\t        .map(|s| s.trim())\n374\t        .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())\n375\t        .map(|directive| {\n376\t            let parts: Vec<&str> = directive.splitn(2, ' ').collect();\n377\t            if parts.len() == 2 {\n378\t                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![parts[1].to_string()])\n379\t            } else {\n380\t                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![])\n381\t            }\n382\t        })\n383\t        .collect();\n384\t\n385\t    // Helper to merge overrides into a directive\n386\t    let merge_into = |directives: &mut Vec<(String, Vec<String>)>,\n387\t                      name: &str,\n388\t                      values: &[String]| {\n389\t        if values.is_empty() {\n390\t            return;\n391\t        }\n392\t        let name_lower = name.to_lowercase();\n393\t        if let Some(entry) = directives.iter_mut().find(|(n, _)| n == &name_lower) {\n394\t            // Append to existing directive\n395\t            entry.1.extend(values.iter().cloned());\n396\t            entry.1.dedup(); // Remove duplicates\n397\t        } else {\n398\t            // Add new directive\n399\t            directives.push((name_lower, values.to_vec()));\n400\t        }\n401\t    };\n402\t\n403\t    // Merge each override category\n404\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"script-src\", &overrides.script_src);\n405\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"img-src\", &overrides.img_src);\n406\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"connect-src\", &overrides.connect_src);\n407\t\n408\t    // Rebuild CSP string\n409\t    directives\n410\t        .into_iter()\n411\t        .map(|(name, values)| {\n412\t            if values.is_empty() {\n413\t                name\n414\t            } else {\n415\t                format!(\"{} {}\", name, values.join(\" \"))\n416\t            }\n417\t        })\n418\t        .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n419\t        .join(\"; \")\n420\t}\n421\t\n422\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n423\t// Dispatch verdict\n424\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n425\t\n426\t/// Result of the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n427\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n428\tpub enum AuthVerdict {\n429\t    /// Request is dispatch-exempt (rule 0); handler decides auth.\n430\t    Exempt,\n431\t    /// Authenticated with the given token kind.\n432\t    Authenticated(TokenKind),\n433\t    /// Bearer token looked like a JWT but failed validation (rule 1).\n434\t    JwtInvalid,\n435\t    /// JWT was signature-valid but scope was insufficient (rule 1).\n436\t    JwtScopeDenied,\n437\t    /// No matching key / missing Authorization (rule 4).\n438\t    InvalidAuth,\n439\t}\n440\t\n441\t/// Which key or token type satisfied authentication.\n442\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n443\tpub enum TokenKind {\n444\t    MasterKey,\n445\t    AdminKey,\n446\t    /// JWT validated against primary or previous secret.\n447\t    Jwt,\n448\t    /// Admin session cookie — sealed session ID validated against task store.\n449\t    AdminSession,\n450\t}\n451\t\n452\timpl AuthVerdict {\n453\t    pub fn is_allowed(&self) -> bool {\n454\t        matches!(self, AuthVerdict::Exempt | AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_))\n455\t    }\n456\t}\n457\t\n458\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n459\t// Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt check\n460\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n461\t\n462\t/// Returns true when `(method, path)` is in the exhaustive dispatch-exempt list\n463\t/// (plan §5 rule 5). Exempt endpoints run their handler directly; rules 1–4\n464\t/// are never consulted.\n465\tpub fn is_dispatch_exempt(method: &Method, path: &str) -> bool {\n466\t    // `GET /health` — unauthenticated liveness probe (Meilisearch-compatible)\n467\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/health\" {\n468\t        return true;\n469\t    }\n470\t\n471\t    // `GET /version` — unauthenticated version endpoint (Meilisearch-compatible)\n472\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/version\" {\n473\t        return true;\n474\t    }\n475\t\n476\t    // `GET /_miroir/ready` — unauthenticated readiness probe (plan §10)\n477\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/_miroir/ready\" {\n478\t        return true;\n479\t    }\n480\t\n481\t    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated public locale fetch\n482\t    if method == Method::GET {\n483\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/\") {\n484\t            // Must have at least one path segment after the prefix\n485\t            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains(\"//\");\n486\t        }\n487\t    }\n488\t\n489\t    // `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body\n490\t    if method == Method::POST && path == \"/_miroir/admin/login\" {\n491\t        return true;\n492\t    }\n493\t\n494\t    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — auth per search_ui.auth.mode\n495\t    if method == Method::GET {\n496\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/\") {\n497\t            let segments: Vec<&str> = rest.split('/').collect();\n498\t            if segments.len() == 2 && segments[1] == \"session\" && !segments[0].is_empty() {\n499\t                return true;\n500\t            }\n501\t        }\n502\t    }\n503\t\n504\t    // `GET /ui/search/{index}` — public SPA entry point\n505\t    if method == Method::GET {\n506\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/ui/search/\") {\n507\t            // Single non-empty segment (the index name)\n508\t            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains('/');\n509\t        }\n510\t    }\n511\t\n512\t    false\n513\t}\n514\t\n515\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n516\t// Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe\n517\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n518\t\n519\t/// Returns true if `token` has the structural shape of a JWT (three\n520\t/// dot-separated base64url segments).\n521\tpub fn probe_jwt_shape(token: &str) -> bool {\n522\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n523\t    if parts.len() != 3 {\n524\t        return false;\n525\t    }\n526\t    // Each segment should be non-empty and look like base64url\n527\t    parts.iter().all(|s| {\n528\t        !s.is_empty()\n529\t            && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '-' || c == '_' || c == '=')\n530\t    })\n531\t}\n532\t\n533\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n534\t// Helper — admin path check\n535\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n536\t\n537\t/// Returns true if `path` starts with `/_miroir/` (admin surface).\n538\tpub fn is_admin_path(path: &str) -> bool {\n539\t    path.starts_with(\"/_miroir/\")\n540\t}\n541\t\n542\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n543\t// Constant-time opaque key comparison\n544\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n545\t\n546\t/// Constant-time comparison of an opaque token against an expected key.\n547\t/// Prevents timing side-channels on secret key values.\n548\tpub fn constant_time_compare(token: &[u8], expected: &[u8]) -> bool {\n549\t    token.ct_eq(expected).into()\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n553\t// Core dispatch — rules 0–4\n554\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n555\t\n556\t/// Execute the full bearer-token dispatch chain for a request.\n557\t///\n558\t/// `bearer_token` is the raw value after stripping `\"Bearer \"` from the\n559\t/// `Authorization` header (may be `None` if the header is absent).\n560\tpub fn dispatch_bearer(\n561\t    method: &Method,\n562\t    path: &str,\n563\t    bearer_token: Option<&str>,\n564\t    state: &AuthState,\n565\t) -> AuthVerdict {\n566\t    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt endpoints skip all auth checks\n567\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(method, path) {\n568\t        return AuthVerdict::Exempt;\n569\t    }\n570\t\n571\t    let token = match bearer_token {\n572\t        Some(t) => t,\n573\t        None => return AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth, // Rule 4 — missing auth\n574\t    };\n575\t\n576\t    // Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe, then full validation\n577\t    if probe_jwt_shape(token) {\n578\t        match state.validate_jwt(token) {\n579\t            Ok(_claims) => return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt),\n580\t            Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty) => {\n581\t                return AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid;\n582\t            }\n583\t            Err(_) => {\n584\t                return AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid;\n585\t            }\n586\t        }\n587\t    }\n588\t\n589\t    // Rule 2 — admin-path opaque-token match\n590\t    if is_admin_path(path) {\n591\t        if constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), state.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n592\t            return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::AdminKey);\n593\t        }\n594\t        return AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth; // Rule 4\n595\t    }\n596\t\n597\t    // Rule 3 — master-key match (non-admin paths)\n598\t    if constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), state.master_key.as_bytes()) {\n599\t        return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::MasterKey);\n600\t    }\n601\t\n602\t    // Rule 4 — mismatch\n603\t    AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth\n604\t}\n605\t\n606\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n607\t// X-Admin-Key short-circuit\n608\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n609\t\n610\t/// Check the `X-Admin-Key` header for admin endpoints.\n611\t/// Returns `true` if the header is present and matches `admin_key`.\n612\t/// Evaluated independently of the bearer chain — short-circuits directly.\n613\tpub fn check_x_admin_key(headers: &HeaderMap, admin_key: &[u8]) -> bool {\n614\t    match headers.get(\"X-Admin-Key\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()) {\n615\t        Some(key) => constant_time_compare(key.as_bytes(), admin_key),\n616\t        None => false,\n617\t    }\n618\t}\n619\t\n620\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n621\t// Axum middleware\n622\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n623\t\n624\t/// Extract the bearer token from `Authorization: Bearer <value>`.\n625\tfn extract_bearer(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<&str> {\n626\t    let auth = headers.get(\"authorization\")?.to_str().ok()?;\n627\t    auth.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \")\n628\t}\n629\t\n630\t/// Extract the sealed admin session cookie value from the Cookie header.\n631\tpub fn extract_admin_session_cookie(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n632\t    let cookie_header = headers.get(\"cookie\")?.to_str().ok()?;\n633\t    for pair in cookie_header.split(';') {\n634\t        let pair = pair.trim();\n635\t        if let Some(value) = pair.strip_prefix(&format!(\"{}=\", admin_session::COOKIE_NAME)) {\n636\t            return Some(value.to_string());\n637\t        }\n638\t    }\n639\t    None\n640\t}\n641\t\n642\t/// Unseal an admin session cookie, returning the session ID.\n643\tpub fn unseal_admin_cookie(cookie_value: &str, key: &SealKey) -> Result<String, admin_session::SealError> {\n644\t    admin_session::unseal_session(cookie_value, key)\n645\t}\n646\t\n647\t/// Axum middleware implementing the bearer-token dispatch chain (plan §5).\n648\tpub async fn auth_middleware(\n649\t    State(state): State<AuthState>,\n650\t    req: Request,\n651\t    next: Next,\n652\t) -> Response {\n653\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n654\t    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n655\t\n656\t    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt: skip everything, handler decides auth\n657\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(&method, &path) {\n658\t        return next.run(req).await;\n659\t    }\n660\t\n661\t    // X-Admin-Key short-circuit for admin endpoints\n662\t    if is_admin_path(&path) && check_x_admin_key(req.headers(), state.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n663\t        return next.run(req).await;\n664\t    }\n665\t\n666\t    // Admin session cookie check for admin endpoints (plan §9, §13.19).\n667\t    // If a sealed admin session cookie is present, unseal it and authenticate.\n668\t    // Revoked sessions are rejected immediately.\n669\t    if is_admin_path(&path) {\n670\t        if let Some(cookie_value) = extract_admin_session_cookie(req.headers()) {\n671\t            match unseal_admin_cookie(&cookie_value, &state.seal_key) {\n672\t                Ok(session_id) => {\n673\t                    // Check revocation cache (populated on logout + Pub/Sub).\n674\t                    if state.revoked_sessions.contains_key(&session_id) {\n675\t                        return MeilisearchError::new(\n676\t                            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n677\t                            \"Admin session has been revoked.\",\n678\t                        )\n679\t                        .into_response();\n680\t                    }\n681\t                    let mut req = req;\n682\t                    req.extensions_mut().insert(AdminSessionId(session_id));\n683\t                    return next.run(req).await;\n684\t                }\n685\t                Err(e) => {\n686\t                    // Cookie tampering or wrong seal key (e.g. cross-pod key\n687\t                    // mismatch in HA).  Log a warning so operators can diagnose\n688\t                    // ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY divergence across pods.\n689\t                    tracing::warn!(\n690\t                        path = %path,\n691\t                        error = %e,\n692\t                        \"admin session cookie unseal failed — tampered cookie or cross-pod key mismatch\"\n693\t                    );\n694\t                }\n695\t            }\n696\t        }\n697\t    }\n698\t\n699\t    // Extract bearer token\n700\t    let bearer = extract_bearer(req.headers());\n701\t\n702\t    // Run the dispatch chain\n703\t    let verdict = dispatch_bearer(&method, &path, bearer, &state);\n704\t\n705\t    match verdict {\n706\t        AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_) | AuthVerdict::Exempt => next.run(req).await,\n707\t        AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid => MeilisearchError::new(\n708\t            MiroirCode::JwtInvalid,\n709\t            \"The provided JWT is invalid or expired.\",\n710\t        )\n711\t        .into_response(),\n712\t        AuthVerdict::JwtScopeDenied => MeilisearchError::new(\n713\t            MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied,\n714\t            \"The provided JWT does not grant access to this resource.\",\n715\t        )\n716\t        .into_response(),\n717\t        AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth => MeilisearchError::new(\n718\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n719\t            \"The provided authorization is invalid.\",\n720\t        )\n721\t        .into_response(),\n722\t    }\n723\t}\n724\t\n725\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n726\t// CSRF validation middleware (plan §9)\n727\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n728\t\n729\t/// CSRF middleware that validates `X-CSRF-Token` on state-changing requests.\n730\t///\n731\t/// Bypasses CSRF check when:\n732\t/// - Request is authenticated via Bearer token (not admin session cookie)\n733\t/// - X-Admin-Key header is present\n734\t/// - Request method is safe (GET, HEAD, OPTIONS)\n735\t/// - Path is dispatch-exempt\n736\t///\n737\t/// For admin session cookie auth, requires `X-CSRF-Token` header to match\n738\t/// the token stored in the session.\n739\tpub async fn csrf_middleware(\n740\t    State(state): State<CsrfState>,\n741\t    req: Request,\n742\t    next: Next,\n743\t) -> Response {\n744\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n745\t    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n746\t\n747\t    // Skip CSRF for safe methods\n748\t    if matches!(method, Method::GET | Method::HEAD | Method::OPTIONS) {\n749\t        return next.run(req).await;\n750\t    }\n751\t\n752\t    // Skip CSRF for non-admin paths\n753\t    if !is_admin_path(&path) {\n754\t        return next.run(req).await;\n755\t    }\n756\t\n757\t    // Skip CSRF for dispatch-exempt endpoints\n758\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(&method, &path) {\n759\t        return next.run(req).await;\n760\t    }\n761\t\n762\t    // Skip CSRF if X-Admin-Key is present (bypasses CSRF)\n763\t    if check_x_admin_key(req.headers(), state.auth.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n764\t        return next.run(req).await;\n765\t    }\n766\t\n767\t    // Check if authenticated via admin session cookie\n768\t    let has_session_cookie = extract_admin_session_cookie(req.headers()).is_some();\n769\t    let has_bearer_token = extract_bearer(req.headers()).is_some();\n770\t\n771\t    // CSRF only applies to session-cookie auth, not bearer tokens\n772\t    if !has_session_cookie || has_bearer_token {\n773\t        return next.run(req).await;\n774\t    }\n775\t\n776\t    // Extract CSRF token from header\n777\t    let csrf_token = match extract_csrf_token(req.headers()) {\n778\t        Some(token) => token,\n779\t        None => {\n780\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n781\t                MiroirCode::MissingCsrf,\n782\t                \"CSRF token is required for state-changing requests.\",\n783\t            )\n784\t            .into_response();\n785\t        }\n786\t    };\n787\t\n788\t    // Get session ID from extensions (set by auth_middleware)\n789\t    let session_id = match req.extensions().get::<AdminSessionId>() {\n790\t        Some(id) => id.0.clone(),\n791\t        None => {\n792\t            // Session cookie was present but auth_middleware didn't set AdminSessionId\n793\t            // This means the session was invalid/expired/revoked\n794\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n795\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n796\t                \"Admin session is invalid or expired.\",\n797\t            )\n798\t            .into_response();\n799\t        }\n800\t    };\n801\t\n802\t    // Validate CSRF token against session\n803\t    let Some(redis_store) = state.redis_store.as_ref() else {\n804\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n805\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n806\t            \"Admin sessions require Redis task store.\",\n807\t        )\n808\t        .into_response();\n809\t    };\n810\t\n811\t    let session = match redis_store.get_admin_session(&session_id) {\n812\t        Ok(Some(s)) => s,\n813\t        Ok(None) => {\n814\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n815\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n816\t                \"Admin session not found.\",\n817\t            )\n818\t            .into_response();\n819\t        }\n820\t        Err(e) => {\n821\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, session_prefix = &session_id[..session_id.len().min(8)], \"failed to get admin session for CSRF validation\");\n822\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n823\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n824\t                \"Failed to validate session.\",\n825\t            )\n826\t            .into_response();\n827\t        }\n828\t    };\n829\t\n830\t    // Check if revoked\n831\t    if session.revoked {\n832\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n833\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n834\t            \"Admin session has been revoked.\",\n835\t        )\n836\t        .into_response();\n837\t    }\n838\t\n839\t    // Check expiration\n840\t    let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n841\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n842\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n843\t        .as_secs() as i64;\n844\t    if session.expires_at < now {\n845\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n846\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n847\t            \"Admin session has expired.\",\n848\t        )\n849\t        .into_response();\n850\t    }\n851\t\n852\t    // Constant-time compare CSRF tokens\n853\t    if !constant_time_csrf_compare(&csrf_token, &session.csrf_token) {\n854\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n855\t            MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch,\n856\t            \"CSRF token does not match the session token.\",\n857\t        )\n858\t        .into_response();\n859\t    }\n860\t\n861\t    next.run(req).await\n862\t}\n863\t\n864\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n865\t// Rate-limit hook types (Phase 2 in-memory stub, Phase 6 multi-pod)\n866\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n867\t\n868\t/// Rate-limit bucket key types wired into the dispatch chain.\n869\t/// Phase 2 keeps these as in-memory counters; Phase 6 will back them\n870\t/// with the task store (Redis/SQLite).\n871\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n872\tpub enum RateLimitBucket {\n873\t    /// `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`\n874\t    AdminLogin(String),\n875\t    /// `miroir:ratelimit:searchui:<ip>`\n876\t    SearchUi(String),\n877\t}\n878\t\n879\t/// In-memory rate limiter (Phase 2 stub). Always returns `Ok(())` — actual\n880\t/// enforcement is deferred to Phase 6 multi-pod. The hook is wired here so\n881\t/// handlers can call `limiter.check()` without cfg-gating.\n882\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n883\tpub struct RateLimiter;\n884\t\n885\timpl RateLimiter {\n886\t    pub fn check(&self, _bucket: &RateLimitBucket) -> Result<(), ()> {\n887\t        Ok(()) // Phase 2: always allow\n888\t    }\n889\t}\n890\t\n891\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n892\t// Helpers\n893\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n894\t\n895\tfn epoch_seconds() -> u64 {\n896\t    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n897\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n898\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n899\t        .as_secs()\n900\t}\n901\t\n902\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n903\t// Tests\n904\t// idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n1220\t            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n1221\t            iat: now - 3600,\n1222\t            exp: now - 100, // expired well beyond 30s leeway\n1223\t        };\n1224\t        let header = JwtHeader {\n1225\t            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n1226\t            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n1227\t            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n1228\t .unwrap();\n1235\t\n1236\t        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n1237\t        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::Expired));\n1238\t    }\n1239\t\n1240\t    #[test]\n1241\t    fn tampered_signature_returns_invalid_signature() {\n1242\t        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n1243\t        let mut token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n1244\t        // Tamper with the signature\n1245\t        let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n1246\t        token = format!(\"{}.{}.tampered_sig\", parts[0], -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1255\t\n1256\t    #[test]\n1257\t    fn rotation_old_token_validates_via_previous_secret() {\n1258\t        let primary =            kid: KID_PREVIOUS.to_string(),\n1273\t            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n1274\t        };\n1275\t        let old_token = jwt_encode(&header, &claims, previous.as_bytes()).unwrap();\n1276\t\n1277\t        // Simulate rotation — new primary, old primary as previous\n1278\t        let state = AuthState {\n1279\t            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n1280\t            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n1281\t            jwt_primary: Some(primary.to_string()),\n1282\t            jwt_previous: Some(previous.to_string()),\n1283\t prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n1287\t            ).unwrap(),\n1288\t        };\n1289\t\n1290\t        // Old token should still validate via previous secret\n1291\t seal(session_id: &str, key: &SealKey) -> Result<Self, SealError> {\n125\t        let cipher = XChaCha20Poly1305::new_from_slice(&key.key)\n126\t            .map_err(|_| SealError::KeyError)?;\n127\t\n128\t        let mut nonce_bytes = [0u8; NONCE_LEN];\n129\t        rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut nonce_bytes);\n130\t        let nonce = XNonce::from_slice(&nonce_bytes);\n131\t\n132\t        let ciphertext = cipher\n133\t            .encrypt(nonce, session_id.as_bytes())\n134\t            .map_err(|_| SealError::EncryptError)?;\n135\t\n136\t        Ok(Self {\n137\t            nonce: nonce_bytes,\n138\t            ciphertext,\n139\t        })\n140\t    }\n141\t\n142\t    /// Unseal a cookie value, returning the plaintext session ID.\n143\t    pub fn unseal(&self, key: &SealKey) -> Result<String, SealError> {\n144\t        let cipher = XChaCha20Poly1305::new_from_slice(&key.key)\n145\t            .map_err(|_| SealError::KeyError)?;\n146\t\n147\t        let nonce = XNonce::from_slice(&self.nonce);\n148\t        let plaintext = cipher\n149\t            .decrypt(nonce, self.ciphertext.as_slice())\n150\t            .map_err(|_| SealError::DecryptError)?;\n151\t\n152\t        String::from_utf8(plaintext).map_err(|_| SealError::InvalidUtf8)\n153\t    }\n154\t\n155\t    /// Encode to base64 for cookie storage.\n156\t    pub fn encode(&self) -> String {\n157\t        let mut buf = Vec::with_capacity(NONCE_LEN + self.ciphertext.len());\n158\t        buf.extend_from_slice(&self.nonce);\n159\t        buf.extend_from_slice(&self.ciphertext);\n160\t        URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(&buf)\n161\t    }\n162\t\n163\t    /// Decode from base64 cookie value.\n164\t    pub fn decode(value: &str) -> Result<Self, SealError> {\n165\t        let bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n166\t            .decode(value)\n167\t            .map_err(|_| SealError::MalformedCookie)?;\n168\t\n169\t        // Minimum size: nonce + 1 byte plaintext + tag\n170\t        if bytes.len() < NONCE_LEN + 1 + TAG_LEN {\n171\t            return Err(SealError::MalformedCookie);\n172\t        }\n173\t\n174\t        let mut nonce = [0u8; NONCE_LEN];\n175\t        nonce.copy_from_slice(&bytes[..NONCE_LEN]);\n176\t        let ciphertext = bytes[NONCE_LEN..].to_vec();\n177\t\n178\t        Ok(Self { nonce, ciphertext })\n179\t    }\n180\t}\n181\t\n182\t/// Seal a session ID into a base64 cookie value.\n183\tpub fn seal_session(session_id: &str, key: &SealKey) -> Result<String, SealError> {\n184\t    let sealed = SealedCookie::seal(session_id, key)?;\n185\t    Ok(sealed.encode())\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\t/// Unseal a base64 cookie value into a session ID.\n189\tpub fn unseal_session(cookie_value: &str, key: &SealKey) -> Result<String, SealError> {\n190\t    let sealed = SealedCookie::decode(cookie_value)?;\n191\t    sealed.unseal(key)\n192\t}\n193\t\n194\t/// Errors during seal/unseal operations.\n195\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n196\tpub enum SealError {\n197\t    /// The seal key is invalid.\n198\t    KeyError,\n199\t    /// Encryption failed.\n200\t    EncryptError,\n201\t    /// Decryption failed — wrong key or tampered ciphertext.\n202\t    DecryptError,\n203\t    /// The cookie value is malformed (wrong length, bad base64).\n204\t    MalformedCookie,\n205\t    /// The decrypted plaintext is not valid UTF-8.\n206\t    InvalidUtf8,\n207\t}\n208\t\n209\timpl std::fmt::Display for SealError {\n210\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n211\t        match self {\n212\t            SealError::KeyError => write!(f, \"invalid seal key\"),\n213\t            SealError::EncryptError => write!(f, \"encryption failed\"),\n214\t            SealError::DecryptError => write!(f, \"decryption failed — wrong key or tampered cookie\"),\n215\t            SealError::MalformedCookie => write!(f, \"malformed cookie value\"),\n216\t            SealError::InvalidUtf8 => write!(f, \"decrypted value is not valid UTF-8\"),\n217\t        }\n218\t    }\n219\t}\n220\t\n221\timpl std::error::Error for SealError {}\n222\t\n223\t#[cfg(test)]\n224\tmod tests {\n225\t    use counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n77\t    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n78\t    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n79\t    group_totals: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n80\t    /// Groups that met quorum: group_id -> true\n81\t    groups_met_quorum: HashMap<u32, bool>,\n82\t    /// Total degraded groups count\n83\t    degraded_groups: u32,\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\timpl QuorumState {\n87\t    /// Record a write attempt to a node.\n88\t    fn record_attempt(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n89\t        *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n93\t    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n94\t        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n95\t    }\n96\t\n97\t    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n98\t    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n99\t        // Track that this group had a failure\n100\t        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n101\t    }\n102\t\n103\t    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n104\t    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n105\t        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n106\t        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n107\t        let met = acks >= quorum;\n108\t        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n109\t        met\n110\t    }\n111\t\n112\t    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n113\t    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n114\t        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n115\t    }\n116\t\n117\t    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n118\t    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n119\t        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n120\t        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n121\t            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n122\t                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n123\t                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n124\t                    degraded += 1;\n125\t                }\n126\t            }\n127\t        }\n128\t        degraded\n129\t    }\n130\t}\n131\t\n132\t/// Build router for document endpoints.\n133\tpub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\n134\twhere\n135\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n136\t{\n137\t    Router::new()\n138\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::post(post_documents))\n139\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::put(put_documents))\n140\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n141\t        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document_by_id))\n142\t}\n143\t\n144\t/// POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - Add documents.\n145\tasync fn post_documents(\n146\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n147\t    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n148\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n149\t    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n150\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n151\t    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state).await\n152\t}\n153\t\n154\t/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace documents.\n155\tasync fn put_documents(\n156\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n157\t    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n158\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n159\t    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n160\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n161\t    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state).await\n162\t}\n163\t\n164\t/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\n165\tasync fn delete_documents(\n166\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n167\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n168\t    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n169\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n170\t    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n171\t    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n172\t        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n173\t            index_uid: index.clone(),\n174\t            filter: filter.clone(),\n175\t        };\n176\t        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state).await;\n177\t    }\n178\t\n179\t    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n180\t    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n181\t        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n182\t            .iter()\n183\t            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n184\t            .collect();\n185\t        if !ids.is_empty() {\n186\t            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n187\t                index_uid: index.clone(),\n188\t                ids,\n189\t            };\n190\t            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state).await;\n191\t        }\n192\t    }\n193\t\n194\t    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n195\t    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n196\t        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n197\t        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n198\t    ))\n199\t}\n200\t\n201\t/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\n202\tasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n203\t    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n204\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n205\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n206\t    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n207\t        index_uid: index.clone(),\n208\t        ids: vec![id],\n209\t    };\n210\t    delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state).await\n211\t}\n212\t\n213\t/// Implementation for write documents (POST/PUT).\n214\tasync fn write_documents_impl(\n215\t    index: String,\n216\t    primary_key: Option<String>,\n217\t    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n218\t    state: &AppState,\n219\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n220\t    if documents.is_empty() {\n221\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n222\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n223\t            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n224\t        ));\n225\t    }\n226\t\n227\t    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n228\t    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n229\t        documents\n230\t            .first()\n231\t            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n232\t    });\n233\t\n234\t    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n235\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n236\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n237\t            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n238\t        )\n239\t    })?;\n240\t\n241\t    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n242\t    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n243\t        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n244\t        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n245\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n246\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n247\t                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n248\t            ));\n249\t        }\n250\t\n251\t        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n252\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n253\t                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n254\t                format!(\n255\t                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n256\t                    i, primary_key\n257\t                ),\n258\t            ));\n259\t        }\n260\t    }\n261\t\n262\t    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard into each document\n263\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n264\t    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n265\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n266\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n267\t\n268\t    for doc in &mut documents {\n269\t        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n270\t            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n271\t            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n272\t        }\n273\t    }\n274\t\n275\t    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n276\t    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n277\t\n278\t    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n279\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n280\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n281\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n282\t    );\n283\t\n284\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n285\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n286\t\n287\t    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n288\t    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n289\t        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n290\t\n291\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n292\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n293\t                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n294\t                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n295\t            ));\n296\t        }\n297\t\n298\t        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n299\t\n300\t        for node_id in targets {\n301\t            let node = topology\n302\t                .node(&node_id)\n303\t                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n304\t\n305\t            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n306\t            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n307\t\n308\t            let req = WriteRequest {\n309\t                index_uid: index.clone(),\n310\t                documents: docs.clone(),\n311\t                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n312\t            };\n313\t\n314\t            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n315\t                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n316\t                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n317\t                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n318\t                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n319\t                    }\n320\t                }\n321\t                Ok(resp) => {\n322\t                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n323\t                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n324\t                }\n325\t                Err(_) => {\n326\t                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n327\t                }\n328\t            }\n329\t        }\n330\t    }\n331\t\n332\t    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n333\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n334\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n335\t\n336\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n337\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n338\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n339\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n340\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n341\t        ));\n342\t    }\n343\t\n344\t    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n345\t    let miroir_task = state\n346\t        .task_registry\n347\t        .register_with_metadata(\n348\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n349\t            Some(index.clone()),\n350\t            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n351\t        )\n352\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n353\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n354\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n355\t        ))?;\n356\t\n357\t    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n358\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n359\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n360\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n361\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n362\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n363\t            error: None,\n364\t            error_type: None,\n365\t            code: None,\n366\t            link: None,\n367\t        },\n368\t        degraded_groups,\n369\t    )\n370\t}\n371\t\n372\t/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\n373\tasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n374\t    index: String,\n375\t    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n376\t    state: &AppState,\n377\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n378\t    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n379\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n380\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n381\t            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n382\t        ));\n383\t    }\n384\t\n385\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n386\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n387\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n388\t\n389\t    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n390\t    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n391\t    for id in &req.ids {\n392\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n393\t        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n394\t    }\n395\t\n396\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n397\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n398\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n399\t    );\n400\t\n401\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n402\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n403\t\n404\t    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n405\t    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n406\t        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n407\t\n408\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n409\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n410\t                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n411\t                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n412\t            ));\n413\t        }\n414\t\n415\t        for node_id in targets {\n416\t            let node = topology\n417\t                .node(&node_id)\n418\t                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n419\t\n420\t            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n421\t            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n422\t\n423\t            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n424\t                index_uid: index.clone(),\n425\t                ids: ids.clone(),\n426\t            };\n427\t\n428\t            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n429\t                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n430\t                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n431\t                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n432\t                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n433\t                    }\n434\t                }\n435\t                Ok(resp) => {\n436\t                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n437\t                }\n438\t                Err(_) => {\n439\t                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n440\t                }\n441\t            }\n442\t        }\n443\t    }\n444\t\n445\t    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n446\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n447\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n448\t\n449\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n450\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n451\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n452\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n453\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n454\t        ));\n455\t    }\n456\t\n457\t    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n458\t    let miroir_task = state\n459\t        .task_registry\n460\t        .register_with_metadata(\n461\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n462\t            Some(index.clone()),\n463\t            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n464\t        )\n465\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n466\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n467\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n468\t        ))?;\n469\t\n470\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n471\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n472\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n473\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n474\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n475\t            error: None,\n476\t            error_type: None,\n477\t            code: None,\n478\t            link: None,\n479\t        },\n480\t        degraded_groups,\n481\t    )\n482\t}\n483\t\n484\t/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\n485\tasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n486\t    index: String,\n487\t    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n488\t    state: &AppState,\n489\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n490\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n491\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n492\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n493\t\n494\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n495\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n496\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n497\t    );\n498\t\n499\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n500\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n501\t\n502\t    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n503\t    for node in topology.nodes() {\n504\t        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n505\t        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n506\t\n507\t        match client\n508\t            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n509\t            .await\n510\t        {\n511\t            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n512\t                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n513\t                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n514\t                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n515\t                }\n516\t            }\n517\t            Ok(resp) => {\n518\t                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n519\t            }\n520\t            Err(_) => {\n521\t                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n522\t            }\n523\t        }\n524\t    }\n525\t\n526\t    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n527\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n528\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n529\t\n530\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n531\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n532\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n533\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n534\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n535\t        ));\n536\t    }\n537\t\n538\t    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n539\t    let miroir_task = state\n540\t        .task_registry\n541\t        .register_with_metadata(\n542\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n543\t            Some(index.clone()),\n544\t            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n545\t        )\n546\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n547\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n548\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n549\t        ))?;\n550\t\n551\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n552\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n553\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n554\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n555\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n556\t            error: None,\n557\t            error_type: None,\n558\t            code: None,\n559\t            link: None,\n560\t        },\n561\t        degraded_groups,\n562\t    )\n563\t}\n564\t\n565\t/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n566\t///\n567\t/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\n568\tfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n569\t    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n570\t        .iter()\n571\t        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n572\t        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n573\t}\n574\t\n575\t/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n576\t///\n577\t/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n578\t/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n579\t///\n580\t/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\n581\tfn group_documents_by_shard(\n582\t    documents: &[Value],\n583\t    primary_key: &str,\n584\t    topology: &Topology,\n585\t) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n586\t    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n587\t\n588\t    for doc in documents {\n589\t        let pk_value = doc\n590\t            .get(primary_key)\n591\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n592\t            .ok_or_else(|| {\n593\t                MeilisearchError::new(\n594\t                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n595\t                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n596\t                )\n597\t            })?;\n598\t\n599\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n600\t        shard_documents\n601\t            .entry(shard_id)\n602\t            .or_default()\n603\t            .push(doc.clone());\n604\t    }\n605\t\n606\t    Ok(shard_documents)\n607\t}\n608\t\n609\t/// Build an error response from a node error.\n610\tfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n611\t    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n612\t        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n613\t        indexUid: None,\n614\t        status: None,\n615\t        error: resp.message,\n616\t        error_type: resp.error_type,\n617\t        code: resp.code,\n618\t        link: None,\n619\t    }\n620\t}\n621\t\n622\t/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\n623\tfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n624\t    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n625\t    degraded_groups: u32,\n626\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n627\t    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n628\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n629\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n630\t            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n631\t        )\n632\t    })?;\n633\t\n634\t    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n635\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n636\t        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n637\t\n638\t    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n639\t    if degraded_groups >         if let Err(e) = check_and_rotate(&state, &index_uid, false).await {\n85\t                error!(index = %index_uid, error = %e, \"scoped key rotation failed\");\n86\t            }\n87\t        }\n88\t\n89\t        // Renew the lease\n90\t        let _ = state.redis.renew_leader_lease(\n91\t            lease_scope,\n92\t            &state.pod_id,\n93\t            now_ms() + }\n202\t\n203\t        // Not all pods have caught up yet\n204\t        if tokio::time::Instant::now() >= drain_deadline {\n205\t            warn!(\n206\t                index = %index_uid,\n207\t {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_ed9a408c95d34d9fa19f747e","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have a thorough understanding of all the relevant files. Let me compile my comprehensive findings.\n\n---\n\n## PII Logging Audit Report for /home/coding/miroir\n\n### SUMMARY\n\nThe codebase demonstrates generally strong PII hygiene. Most logging calls are well-structured and avoid leaking sensitive values. However, I found several issues of varying severity, plus one important MISSING structured logging field.\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE 1: Raw URI Path Logged in Middleware Span (MEDIUM severity)\n\n**File:** `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/middleware.rs`\n**Line:** 839\n\n```rust\npath = %req.uri().path(),\n```\n\nThe middleware span at line 833-840 logs the raw request URI path (including the actual index name, e.g. `/indexes/my-sensitive-index/search`). While `path_template` is correctly used for the Prometheus metrics labels and the structured log events (lines 871-903), the span itself also carries `path = %req.uri().path()`. This means the actual path (with index names and document IDs embedded in URL segments) is present in every span that inherits from the middleware span. Any child log event or error that references the span context will include the raw path.\n\nThis is not a query-string leak per se, but it does expose index names in every log line that inherits the span. Index names can be considered PII-adjacent if they contain customer names or internal identifiers.\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE 2: `AdminLoginRequest` Derives `Debug` with `admin_key` Field (LOW severity)\n\n**File:** `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/session.rs`\n**Line:** 39-42\n\n```rust\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct AdminLoginRequest {\n    pub admin_key: String,\n}\n```\n\nThe `admin_key` field contains the actual admin secret. The `#[derive(Debug)]` means that if this struct is ever passed through `{:?}` formatting or if a developer adds `dbg!(&body)` or logs the struct with `Debug` formatting, the admin key would appear in plaintext in logs. Currently no code path actually logs this struct, so this is a latent risk rather than an active leak. The `Debug` impl should be custom-implemented to redact the key.\n\nA similar latent risk exists for:\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/search.rs` line 62-73: `SearchRequestBody` derives `Debug` and contains `q: Option<String>` (the user's search query) and `filter: Option<Value>` and `rest: Value`. If ever logged with `{:?}`, the full query and filter would be exposed. The `#[instrument(skip_all)]` on the handler (line 86) correctly skips all arguments, so there is no active leak.\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE 3: Cookie Unseal Failure Logs Raw Path (LOW severity)\n\n**File:** `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/auth.rs`\n**Line:** 689-693\n\n```rust\ntracing::warn!(\n    path = %path,\n    error = %e,\n    \"admin session cookie unseal failed -- tampered cookie or cross-pod key mismatch\"\n);\n```\n\nThe `path` logged here is the raw request path, which may include index names or document IDs from non-admin endpoints. Since this only fires for `/_miroir/` admin paths, the risk is minimal since admin paths generally do not embed user-provided sensitive data in the URL.\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE 4: CSRF Validation Logs Session ID Prefix (LOW severity -- by design)\n\n**File:** `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/auth.rs`\n**Line:** 821\n\n```rust\ntracing::warn!(error = %e, session_prefix = &session_id[..session_id.len().min(8)], \"failed to get admin session for CSRF validation\");\n```\n\nThe first 8 characters of the session ID are logged. This is intentional for correlation purposes (the session prefix function is used throughout `session.rs` at lines 34-36 and appears at lines 165, 201, 245, 276, 280). While the prefix alone is not sufficient to hijack a session, it does leak partial session identifiers. This is an accepted trade-off for debuggability.\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE 5: `#[instrument]` on Search Handler is Safe (NO ISSUE -- CONFIRMED GOOD)\n\n**File:** `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/search.rs`\n**Line:** 86\n\n```rust\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n```\n\nThe `skip_all` directive correctly prevents the `body: SearchRequestBody` (which contains the query `q`, filters, etc.) from being logged by the instrumentation macro. Only the `index` name is explicitly added as a field.\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE 6: Search Debug Log Correctly Avoids Query PII (NO ISSUE -- CONFIRMED GOOD)\n\n**File:** `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/search.rs`\n**Lines:** 231-239\n\n```rust\ntracing::debug!(\n    target: \"miroir.search\",\n    index = %index,\n    node_count = node_count,\n    estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n    degraded = result.degraded,\n    duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n    \"search completed\"\n);\n```\n\nThis structured log correctly includes: `index`, `node_count`, `estimated_hits`, `degraded`, `duration_ms`. It does NOT log the query string (`q`), filter values, facet values, document content, or any hit data. This means the actual path (with index names and document IDs embedded in URL segments) is present in every span that inherits from the middleware span. The `#[derive(Debug)]` means that if this struct is ever passed through `{:?}` formatting or if a developer adds `dbg!(&body)` or logs the struct with `Debug` formatting, the admin key would appear in plaintext in logs. Currently no code path actually logs this struct, so this is a latent risk rather than an active leak. The `Debug` impl should be custom-implemented to redact the key.\n\nA similar latent risk exists for:\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/search.rs` line 62-73: `SearchRequestBody` derives `Debug` and contains `q: Option<String>` (the user's search query) and `filter: Option<Value>` and `rest: Value`.
